Paragraph.Text: the nature of the principal business of an entity referred to in paragraph (a), the entity’s registration or incorporation number and the jurisdiction and country of issue of that number;

Paragraph.Label: (a.3)
Paragraph.Text: the following information in respect of a person or entity referred to in paragraph (a):

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: their address, telephone number and electronic mail address,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: any identification number assigned to them by a person or entity who made a report referred to in subsection 55(1),

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the Uniform Resource Locator of their website, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the type of document or other information used to identify or verify their identity, the jurisdiction and country of issue of the document and the number of the document or the number associated with the information;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 55.1(3)(c) to (d.1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: the purpose of the transaction, attempted transaction, importation or exportation;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an importation or exportation, the amount and type of currency or monetary instruments;

Paragraph.Label: (c.1)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a transaction or attempted transaction,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount and type of currency, monetary instruments or virtual currency involved, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if no currency, monetary instruments or virtual currency is involved, the value of the transaction or attempted transaction or the type and value of the funds or other remittances that are the subject of the transaction or attempted transaction;

Paragraph.Label: (c.2)
Paragraph.Text: the rate of exchange used in relation to the transaction, attempted transaction, importation or exportation;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a transaction or attempted transaction,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the manner in which the transaction was conducted or the attempted transaction was to be conducted,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: any transaction number, account number, institution number, branch number or similar identifying number involved,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the date on which any account involved is opened or closed, as well as its status,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the posting date,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: any identification number assigned to a person or entity referred to in paragraph (a) as part of the transaction or attempted transaction, and

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: the bank identification code or business entity identifier of any person or entity referred to in paragraph (a) that is a member of the Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication;

Paragraph.Label: (d.1)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a transaction or attempted transaction involving virtual currency or an electronic funds transfer as defined in the Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Regulations, transaction identifiers, including sending and receiving addresses, and any user name of a person or entity referred to in paragraph (a);

Subsection.Text: Subsection 55.1(3) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (d.2):

Paragraph.Label: (d.3)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a transaction or attempted transaction that, in whole or in part, is conducted online, the type of device used to conduct the transaction or attempted transaction, as well as the date and time of the transaction or attempted transaction;

Paragraph.Label: (d.4)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a transaction, whether it was completed or not;

Paragraph.Label: (d.5)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an incomplete transaction or an attempted transaction, the reason it was not completed;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 55.1(3) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (h):

Paragraph.Label: (h.1)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a transaction or attempted transaction involving an electronic funds transfer as defined in the Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Regulations, any information in respect of the relationships that exist between any persons or entities connected in any way to the transaction or attempted transaction, including any person or entity that initiates or may benefit from it;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 56.1(5)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the name of any person or entity that is involved in the transaction, attempted transaction, importation or exportation or of any person or entity acting on their behalf;

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: the date of birth, gender, country of residence and nature of the occupation or business of a person referred to in paragraph (a), any alias that they use or have used and the name and business address of their employer;

Paragraph.Label: (a.2)
Paragraph.Text: the nature of the principal business of an entity referred to in paragraph (a), the entity’s registration or incorporation number and the jurisdiction and country of issue of that number;

Paragraph.Label: (a.3)
Paragraph.Text: the following information in respect of a person or entity referred to in paragraph (a):

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: their address, telephone number and electronic mail address,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: any identification number assigned to them by a person or entity who made a report referred to in subsection 55(1),

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the Uniform Resource Locator of their website, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the type of document or other information used to identify or verify their identity, the jurisdiction and country of issue of the document and the number of the document or the number associated with the information;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 56.1(5)(c) to (d.1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: the purpose of the transaction, attempted transaction, importation or exportation;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an importation or exportation, the amount and type of currency or monetary instruments;

Paragraph.Label: (c.1)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a transaction or attempted transaction,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount and type of currency, monetary instruments or virtual currency involved, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if no currency, monetary instruments or virtual currency is involved, the value of the transaction or attempted transaction or the type and value of the funds or other remittances that are the subject of the transaction or attempted transaction;

Paragraph.Label: (c.2)
Paragraph.Text: the rate of exchange used in relation to the transaction, attempted transaction, importation or exportation;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a transaction or attempted transaction,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the manner in which the transaction was conducted or the attempted transaction was to be conducted,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: any transaction number, account number, institution number, branch number or similar identifying number involved,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the date on which any account involved is opened or closed, as well as its status,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the posting date,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: any identification number assigned to a person or entity referred to in paragraph (a) as part of the transaction or attempted transaction, and

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: the bank identification code or business entity identifier of any person or entity referred to in paragraph (a) that is a member of the Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication;

Paragraph.Label: (d.1)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a transaction or attempted transaction involving virtual currency or an electronic funds transfer as defined in the Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Regulations, transaction identifiers, including sending and receiving addresses, and any user name of a person or entity referred to in paragraph (a);

Subsection.Text: Subsection 56.1(5) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (d.2):

Paragraph.Label: (d.3)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a transaction or attempted transaction that, in whole or in part, is conducted online, the type of device used to conduct the transaction or attempted transaction, as well as the date and time of the transaction or attempted transaction;

Paragraph.Label: (d.4)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a transaction, whether it was completed or not;

Paragraph.Label: (d.5)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an incomplete transaction or an attempted transaction, the reason it was not completed;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 56.1(5) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (h):

Paragraph.Label: (h.1)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a transaction or attempted transaction involving an electronic funds transfer as defined in the Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Regulations, any information in respect of the relationships that exist between any persons or entities connected in any way to the transaction or attempted transaction, including any person or entity that initiates or may benefit from it;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 58(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: conduct research into trends and developments in the area of money laundering, the financing of terrorist activities and the financing of threats to the security of Canada and into improved ways of detecting, preventing and deterring money laundering, the financing of terrorist activities and the financing of threats to the security of Canada; and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 58(1)(c) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of subparagraph (ii.1) and by adding the following after subparagraph (ii.1):

Subparagraph.Label: (ii.2)
Subparagraph.Text: the nature and extent of the financing, inside and outside Canada, of threats to the security of Canada, and

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 58(1)(c)(iii) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: measures that have been or might be taken to detect, prevent and deter the money laundering — as well as the financing of terrorist activities and the financing of threats to the security of Canada — inside or outside Canada, and the effectiveness of those measures.

Section.Text: Subsection 58.1(2) of the Act is repealed.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 63:

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of sections 62 and 63, an authorized person is considered to have entered a place when accessing it remotely by a means of telecommunication.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Limitation — access by means of telecommunication
Subsection.Text: An authorized person who enters remotely, by a means of telecommunication, a place that is not accessible to the public shall do so with the knowledge of the owner or person in charge of the place and shall be remotely in the place for no longer than the period necessary for the purpose referred to in subsection 62(1).

Section.Text: Subsection 65(3) of the Act is repealed.
Subsection 73.13(1) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every contravention that is designated under paragraph 73.1(1)(a) constitutes a violation and the person or entity that commits the violation is liable to a penalty determined in accordance with sections 73.1 and 73.11.

Section.Text: Subsection 73.15(1) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If the person or entity pays the penalty proposed in the notice of violation, the person or entity is deemed to have committed the violation and proceedings in respect of it are ended.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 77.1:

Subsection.Text: Every person or entity that is an employer, that acts on behalf of an employer or that is in a position of authority in respect of an employee commits an offence if the person or entity takes a disciplinary measure against, demotes, terminates or otherwise adversely affects the employment of an employee, or threatens to do so,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: with the intent to compel the employee to abstain from fulfilling an obligation under this Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: with the intent to retaliate against the employee because the employee has fulfilled or taken steps to fulfill any such obligation.

Subsection.Text: Every person or entity that commits an offence under subsection (1)

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an offence punishable on summary conviction; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than five years.

Subsection.Text: Every person or entity commits an offence that directly or indirectly undertakes, or attempts to undertake, a structured financial transaction.
For the purpose of subsection (1), a structured financial transaction is a series of financial transactions that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: cause a person or entity referred to in section 5 to be in receipt of cash or virtual currency or involve the initiation of an international electronic funds transfer;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: would, if they occurred as a single financial transaction, require a person or entity referred to in section 5 to report it to the Centre; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: are undertaken with the intent that a person or entity referred to in section 5 will not report a financial transaction to the Centre.

Subsection.Text: Every person or entity that is guilty of an offence under subsection (1) is liable

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: on summary conviction, to a fine or imprisonment for a term of not more than two years less a day, or to both; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on conviction on indictment, to a fine or imprisonment for a term of not more than five years, or to both.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 77.3:
A person or entity referred to in section 11.1 that knowingly engages in an activity for which it is not registered with the Centre is guilty of an offence and liable

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: on summary conviction, to a fine of not more than $250,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than two years less a day, or to both; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on conviction on indictment, to a fine of not more than $500,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than five years, or to both.

Subsection.Text: Section 81 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Proceedings under paragraph 74(1)(a), 74(2)(a), 75(1)(a) or 76(a), subsection 77(1) or (2) or paragraph 77.1(a) or 77.2(2)(a) may be instituted within, but not after, five years after the time when the subject-matter of the proceedings arose.
Proceedings under paragraph 77.3(3)(a) may be instituted within, but not after, eight years after the time when the subject-matter of the proceedings arose.
Subsection 81(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Proceedings under paragraph 77.3(3)(a) or 77.4(2)(a) may be instituted within, but not after, eight years after the time when the subject-matter of the proceedings arose.

Section.Text: The headings before section 83 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Heading.Label: PART 6
Heading.TitleText: Transitional Provisions, Consequential and Conditional Amendments, Repeal and Coming into Force

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 83:
The following definitions apply in this section and in sections 83.2 and 83.3.

Definition.Text: agent or mandatary, in relation to a specified person or entity, means any agent or mandatary acting on the commencement day on behalf of the specified person or entity to provide any of the services referred to in paragraph 5(h). (mandataire)
commencement day means the day on which this section and sections 83.2 and 83.3 come into force. (date de référence)
second anniversary means the second anniversary of the commencement day. (deuxième anniversaire)
specified person or entity means a person or entity referred to in paragraph 5(h). (personne ou entité déterminée)

Subsection.Text: A specified person or entity shall, no later than the second anniversary,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: verify whether every agent or mandatary is a person or entity referred to in any of paragraphs 11.11(1)(a) to (f); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: obtain and review the documents referred to in subsection 9.93(2) in respect of every agent or mandatary.

Subsection.Text: The verification and review carried out under subsection (1) are deemed to be a verification carried out under section 9.92 and a review carried out under subsection 9.93(1), respectively.

A specified person or entity that is registered with the Centre on the commencement day shall, in the prescribed form and manner, provide the Centre with the documents referred to in paragraph 11.12(1)(b) or (c) no later than the second anniversary.
The obligation to provide a translation set out in subsection 11.12(1.1) applies with respect to those documents.

Section.Text: The Budget Implementation Act, 2021, No. 1 is amended by adding the following after section 175:

Section.MarginalNote: No ascertainment of expenses in first year
Section.Text: The Financial Transactions and Reports Analysis Centre of Canada is not required to ascertain, under subsection 51.1(1) of the Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering and Terrorist Financing) Act, as enacted by section 165, the total amount of prescribed expenses incurred during the fiscal year in which section 165 comes into force.
Subsection 176(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Sections 164, 165 and 170 come into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

Section.Text: On the first day on which both subsection 193(1) of this Act is in force and section 185 the Budget Implementation Act, 2021, No. 1 has produced its effects, the portion of subsection 55(1) of the Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering and Terrorist Financing) Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (3) and (6.1), sections 52, 53.1, 53.31 to 53.6, 55.1, 56.1 and 56.2, subsection 58(1) and sections 58.1, 65 to 65.1 and 68.1 of this Act and to subsection 12(1) of the Privacy Act, the Centre shall not disclose the following:
Section 181 comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Sections 182, 185, 186, 205 and 206 come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Section 192 comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Section 203 and subsection 204(2) come into force on the first anniversary of the day on which this Act receives royal assent.

Section.Text: Subsection 462.32(4.1) of the French version of the Criminal Code is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Sous réserve de la présente loi et de toute autre loi fédérale, l’agent de la paix qui a saisi une chose en vertu d’un mandat délivré par un juge en vertu du présent article peut, avec le consentement du procureur général donné par écrit, restituer la chose saisie, sur réception d’un reçu à cet effet, à la personne qui a droit à la possession légitime de celle-ci si, à la fois :

Paragraph.Label: a)
Paragraph.Text: il est convaincu qu’il n’y a aucune contestation quant à la possession légitime de la chose saisie;

Paragraph.Label: b)
Paragraph.Text: il est convaincu que la détention de la chose saisie n’est pas nécessaire aux fins d’une confiscation;

Paragraph.Label: c)
Paragraph.Text: la chose saisie est restituée avant le dépôt d’un rapport auprès du greffier du tribunal en vertu de l’alinéa (4)b).

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 462.32:

Subsection.Text: If, on an application of the Attorney General, a judge is satisfied by information on oath in Form 1, varied to suit the case, that there are reasonable grounds to believe that any digital assets, including virtual currency, may be the subject of an order of forfeiture made under subsection 462.37(1) or (2.01) or 462.38(2) in respect of a designated offence alleged to have been committed within the province in which the judge has jurisdiction, the judge may issue a warrant authorizing a person named in the warrant or a peace officer to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: search for the digital assets by using a computer program, as defined in subsection 342.1(2); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: seize — including by taking control of the right to access — the digital assets, as well as any other digital assets found during that search that the person or peace officer believes, on reasonable grounds, may be the subject of such an order of forfeiture.

Subsection.Text: The warrant shall contain any terms and conditions that the judge considers advisable to ensure that any search or seizure authorized by the warrant is reasonable in the circumstances.

An application for a warrant under subsection (1) may be made ex parte, shall be made in writing and shall include a statement as to whether any previous applications have been made under subsection (1) with respect to the property that is the subject of the application.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Detention and record of property seized
Subsection.Text: Every person who executes a warrant issued by a judge under this section shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: detain or cause to be detained any seized property, taking reasonable care to ensure that it is preserved so that it may be dealt with in accordance with the law;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: as soon as practicable after the execution of the warrant, cause the following to be sent to the usual place of residence of any person from whom the property is seized if that usual place of residence is in Canada and is known to the person executing the warrant:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a copy of the warrant, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a notice in Form 5.1, varied to suit the case, setting out the address of the court from which a copy of a report on the seized property may be obtained;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: as soon as practicable after the execution of the warrant but within a period of not more than seven days, prepare a report in Form 5.3, varied to suit the case, identifying the seized property and the manner in which it is being detained, and cause the report to be filed with the clerk of the court; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: cause a copy of the report to be provided, on request, to the person from whom the property was seized and to any other person who, in the opinion of the judge, appears to have a valid interest in the seized property.

Subsection.Text: Subject to this or any other Act of Parliament, a peace officer or a person acting on their behalf may, with the written consent of the Attorney General, on being issued a receipt for it, cause the seized property to be returned to the person lawfully entitled to its possession, if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the peace officer is satisfied that there is no dispute as to who is lawfully entitled to possession of the seized property;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the peace officer is satisfied that the continued detention of the seized property is not required for the purpose of forfeiture; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the seized property is returned before a report is filed with the clerk of the court under paragraph (4)(c).

Subsection.Text: Before issuing a warrant under this section in relation to any property, a judge may require notice to be given to and may hear any person who, in the opinion of the judge, appears to have a valid interest in the property unless the judge is of the opinion that giving such notice before the issuance of the warrant would result in the disappearance, dissipation or reduction in value of the property or otherwise affect the property so that all or a part of it could not be seized under the warrant.

Before issuing a warrant under this section, a judge shall require the Attorney General to give any undertakings that the judge considers appropriate with respect to the payment of damages or costs, or both, in relation to the issuance and execution of the warrant.

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 462.331(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: With respect to property seized under section 462.32 or 462.321 or restrained under section 462.33, other than a controlled substance, within the meaning of the Controlled Drugs and Substances Act, or cannabis, as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Cannabis Act, on application of the Attorney General or of any other person with the written consent of the Attorney General, if a judge is of the opinion that the circumstances so require, the judge may
Subsection 462.34(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Application for review of special warrants and restraint orders

Subsection.Text: Any person who has an interest in property that was seized under a warrant issued under section 462.32 or 462.321 or in respect of which a restraint order was made under subsection 462.33(3) may, at any time, apply to a judge for

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an order under subsection (4);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: permission to examine any seized property that does not consist of digital assets; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an order requiring an accounting of any seized digital assets.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 462.34(6) of the English version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

An order under paragraph (4)(b) in respect of property may be made by a judge if the judge is satisfied that the property will no longer be required for the purpose of any investigation or as evidence in any proceeding and
The portion of paragraph 462.34(6)(a) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that a warrant should not have been issued under section 462.32 or 462.321 or a restraint order under subsection 462.33(3) should not have been made in respect of that property, in the case where the application is made by

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 462.34(6)(a) of the English version of the Act after subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: any person who acquired title to or a right of possession of that property from a person referred to in subparagraph (i) under circumstances that give rise to a reasonable inference that the title or right was transferred from that person for the purpose of avoiding the forfeiture of the property; or

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 462.34(6) of the English version of the Act after paragraph (b) is repealed.

Section.Text: Section 462.341 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection 462.34(2), paragraph 462.34(4)(c) and subsections 462.34(5), (5.1) and (5.2) apply, with any modifications that the circumstances require, to a person who has an interest in money, bank-notes or virtual currency or other digital assets that are seized under this Act, the Controlled Drugs and Substances Act or the Cannabis Act and in respect of which proceedings may be taken under subsection 462.37(1) or (2.01) or 462.38(2).
Subsection 462.35(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Expiration of special warrants and restraint orders

Subsection.Text: Subject to this section, if property has been seized under a warrant issued under section 462.32 or 462.321 or a restraint order has been made under section 462.33 in relation to property, the property may be detained or the order may continue in force, as the case may be, for a period not exceeding six months from the seizure or the making of the order, as the case may be.

Section.Text: Section 462.36 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Forwarding to clerk where accused to stand trial
Section.Text: If a judge issues a warrant under section 462.32 or 462.321 or makes a restraint order under section 462.33 in respect of any property, the clerk of the court shall, when an accused is ordered to stand trial for a designated offence, cause to be forwarded to the clerk of the court to which the accused has been ordered to stand trial a copy of the report filed under paragraph 462.32(4)(b) or 462.321(4)(c) or of the restraint order in respect of the property.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 462.43(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Residual disposal of property seized or dealt with under special warrants or restraint orders

Subsection.Text: If property has been seized under a warrant issued under section 462.32 or 462.321, a restraint order has been made under section 462.33 in relation to any property or a recognizance has been entered into under paragraph 462.34(4)(a) in relation to any property and a judge, on application made to the judge by the Attorney General or any person having an interest in the property or on the judge’s own motion, after notice given to the Attorney General and any other person having an interest in the property, is satisfied that the property will no longer be required for the purpose of section 462.37, 462.38 or any other provision of this or any other Act of Parliament respecting forfeiture or for the purpose of any investigation or as evidence in any proceeding, the judge
The portion of paragraph 462.43(1)(c) of the English version of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of property seized under a warrant issued under section 462.32 or 462.321 or property under the control of a person appointed under paragraph 462.331(1)(a),

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 462.48(1.1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an offence against subsection 119(1), section 120, subsection 121(1) or (2), section 122 or subsection 123(1) or (2), or a conspiracy or an attempt to commit, or being an accessory after the fact in relation to, such an offence;

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: an offence against subsection 279.01(1), 279.011(1) or 279.02(1) or (2) or a conspiracy or an attempt to commit, or being an accessory after the fact in relation to, such an offence;

Paragraph.Label: (b.2)
Paragraph.Text: an offence against subsection 346(1) or a conspiracy or an attempt to commit, or being an accessory after the fact in relation to, such an offence;

Paragraph.Label: (b.3)
Paragraph.Text: an offence against paragraph 380(1)(a) or subsection 380(2) or a conspiracy or an attempt to commit, or being an accessory after the fact in relation to, such an offence;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 462.48(1.1) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of paragraph (c) and by adding the following after paragraph (d):

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: an offence against subsection 3(1) of the Corruption of Foreign Public Officials Act, or a conspiracy or an attempt to commit, or being an accessory after the fact in relation to, such an offence; or

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: an offence against section 354, 355.2, 355.4 or 462.31 — or a conspiracy or attempt to commit, or being an accessory after the fact in relation to such an offence — if the offence is alleged to have been committed in relation to any property, thing or proceeds obtained or derived directly or indirectly as a result of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the commission in Canada of an offence referred to in any of paragraphs (a) to (e), or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: an act or omission anywhere that, if it had occurred in Canada, would have constituted an offence referred to in any of those paragraphs.

Section.Text: Subsection 487.1(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (j):

Paragraph.Label: (j.1)
Paragraph.Text: a warrant under subsection 462.321(1);

Section.Text: Paragraph 19.7(3)(b) of the Parliament of Canada Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an order for a special warrant under section 462.32 or 462.321,

Section.Text: Paragraph 52.7(3)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an order for a special warrant under section 462.32 or 462.321,

Heading.TitleText: Mutual Legal Assistance in Criminal Matters Act

Section.Text: Subsection 9.1(3) of the Mutual Legal Assistance in Criminal Matters Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: On being filed, the order may be enforced as if it were a warrant issued under subsection 462.32(1) or 462.321(1) of the Criminal Code or an order made under subsection 462.33(3) of that Act.

Section.Text: Paragraph 9.3(4)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an order for the seizure of proceeds of crime may be enforced as if it were a warrant issued under subsection 462.32(1) or 462.321(1) of the Criminal Code;

Section.Text: Paragraph 4(1)(a) of the Seized Property Management Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)

Section.Text: Paragraph 13(3)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)

Section.Text: Paragraph 16(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to pay claims arising from undertakings given by the Attorney General under subsections 462.32(6), 462.321(7) and 462.33(7) of the Criminal Code, and

Section.Text: This Subdivision comes into force on the 90th day after the day on which this Act receives royal assent.

Heading.Label: DIVISION 4
Heading.TitleText: Preferential Tariff Programs for Developing Countries

Section.Text: The heading before section 17 and sections 17 and 18 of the Customs Tariff are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this Act, goods are shipped directly to Canada from another country when the goods are shipped to Canada from that other country in accordance with the regulations.
For the purposes of determining whether goods are shipped directly to Canada, the Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Minister, make regulations respecting the shipping of goods.

Section.Text: Paragraph 24(1)(b) of the Act is amended by adding the following after subparagraph (ii):

Subparagraph.Label: (ii.1)
Subparagraph.Text: paragraph 36.2(1)(a),

Section.Text: Section 27 of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: GPTP refers to the General Preferential Tariff Plus. (TPGP)

Section.Text: Section 36 of the Act is replaced by the following:
Sections 33 to 35 cease to have effect on December 31, 2034 or on any earlier date that may be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

Subsection.Text: Subject to sections 24 and 36.3 and any order made under section 36.2, goods that originate in a country set out in the List of Countries as a beneficiary of the General Preferential Tariff Plus are entitled to the General Preferential Tariff Plus rates of customs duty.

If “A” is set out in the column entitled “Preferential Tariff” in the List of Tariff Provisions following the abbreviation “GPTP” in relation to goods entitled to the General Preferential Tariff Plus, the General Preferential Tariff Plus rate of customs duty that applies to those goods is the final rate.

If “F” is set out in the column entitled “Preferential Tariff” in the List of Tariff Provisions following the abbreviation “GPTP” in relation to goods entitled to the General Preferential Tariff Plus, the General Preferential Tariff Plus rate of customs duty that applies to those goods is the initial rate, reduced as provided in the “F” Staging List.
The Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Minister, by order, amend the schedule to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: extend entitlement to the General Preferential Tariff Plus to any goods that originate in a country that is a beneficiary of the General Preferential Tariff, if, in the opinion of the Governor in Council, that country conforms to international norms relating to sustainable development and labour and human rights;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: withdraw entitlement to the General Preferential Tariff Plus from any goods that originate in a country that is a beneficiary of that Tariff; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: reduce a rate of customs duty set out following the abbreviation “GPTP” in the column entitled “Preferential Tariff” in the List of Tariff Provisions and the “F” Staging List.

Subsection.Text: An order made under subsection (1)

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: must specify the date on which the order becomes effective;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: must, if the order partially extends entitlement to the General Preferential Tariff Plus, indicate the goods to which that Tariff is extended;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: may exempt the goods from the conditions set out in subsection 24(1) and prescribe any conditions that apply; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: must, if the order wholly or partially withdraws entitlement to the General Preferential Tariff Plus, indicate the goods to which the General Preferential Tariff applies as a consequence.

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Minister, by order, apply a tariff rate quota in respect of goods imported from one or more countries entitled to the General Preferential Tariff Plus for a period specified in the order.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Tariff treatment if tariff rate quota exceeded
Subsection.Text: Goods imported in excess of a tariff rate quota applied under an order under subsection (1) are subject to the tariff treatment that would be applicable to those goods if they were not entitled to the General Preferential Tariff Plus.

Section.Text: Sections 36.1 to 36.3 cease to have effect on December 31, 2034 or on any earlier date that may be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Section 40 of the Act is replaced by the following:
Sections 37 to 39 cease to have effect on December 31, 2034 or on any earlier date that may be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Section 229 comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

Heading.Label: DIVISION 5
Heading.TitleText: Removal of Most-Favoured-Nation Tariff Treatment for Belarus and Russia

Section.Text: The List of Countries and Applicable Tariff Treatments set out in the schedule to the Customs Tariff is amended by deleting the symbol “X” in the column under the heading “MFN” opposite “Belarus” and “Russia” in the column under the heading “Country Name”.
Section 235 comes into force, or is deemed to have come into force, on the day after the day on which the Most-Favoured-Nation Tariff Withdrawal Order (2022-2) ceases to have effect.

Heading.Label: DIVISION 6
Heading.TitleText: Non-application of Sections 27 and 27.1 of the Bank of Canada Act

Section.MarginalNote: Losses — Government of Canada Bond Purchase Program
Section.Text: Despite sections 27 and 27.1 of the Bank of Canada Act, any ascertained surplus of the Bank of Canada during a financial year must be applied to the Bank of Canada’s retained earnings until the earlier of the following events occurs:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Bank of Canada’s retained earnings are equal to zero, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the ascertained surplus applied to the Bank of Canada’s retained earnings is equal to its losses arising from the purchase of Government of Canada securities as part of the Government of Canada Bond Purchase Program from April 1, 2020 to April 25, 2022.

Section.Text: The Canada Innovation Corporation Act is enacted as follows:

BillInternal.LongTitle: An Act respecting the Canada Innovation Corporation

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Canada Innovation Corporation Act.
The following definitions apply in this Act.

Definition.Text: appropriate Minister has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Financial Administration Act. (ministre compétent)
Board means the board of directors of the Corporation. (conseil)
Corporation means the Canada Innovation Corporation continued under section 5. (Corporation)
federal institution means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a department, as defined in section 2 of the Financial Administration Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a Crown corporation, as defined in subsection 83(1) of that Act. (institution fédérale)

Definition.Text: Minister means the Minister of Industry or, if another federal minister is designated under section 4, that minister.‍ (ministre)

Section.Text: In the event of any inconsistency between this Act and Part X of the Financial Administration Act, this Act prevails to the extent of the inconsistency.
The Governor in Council may, by order, designate any federal minister to be the Minister referred to in this Act.
The Canada Innovation Corporation, incorporated under the Canada Business Corporations Act, is continued as a corporation under this Act.
The head office of the Corporation is to be at a place in Canada that is designated by the Governor in Council.
The Corporation is not an agent of His Majesty in right of Canada, except when

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: providing advice to a federal institution at the request of the appropriate Minister;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: developing, delivering or administering programs under paragraph 10(e);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: negotiating arrangements or agreements or carrying out any activities related to such arrangements or agreements under paragraph 10(f); or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: carrying out any activity conducive to the carrying out of its purpose that the Governor in Council may, by order, specify.

Section.Text: In carrying out its purpose, the Corporation has the capacity, rights, powers and privileges of a natural person.

The purpose of the Corporation is to maximize business investment in research and development across all sectors of the economy and in all regions of Canada to promote innovation-driven economic growth.
In carrying out its purpose, the Corporation may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: act as a centre of expertise on national and international industrial and technology trends;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: promote the ownership and retention of intangible assets in Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: monitor, analyze and disseminate information on research and development in Canada, including for the purposes of supporting the evaluation of programs related to its purpose and improving those programs;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: provide financial support, including in the form of grants, or advice;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: at the request of the appropriate Minister, made after consultation by the appropriate Minister with the Minister, develop, deliver or administer a program on behalf of a federal institution to the extent that the Corporation is able to recover the incurred costs that are specified by the Board;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: at the request of the appropriate Minister, made after consultation by the appropriate Minister with the Minister, negotiate arrangements or agreements with foreign entities on behalf of a federal institution or carry out any activities related to such arrangements or agreements on behalf of a federal institution; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: carry out any other function conducive to the carrying out of its purpose that the Governor in Council may, by order, specify.

Heading.TitleText: Board, Chief Executive Officer and Employees

Section.Text: The Board consists of a chairperson and not fewer than four and not more than 11 other directors.

Subsection.Text: Each director, other than the chairperson, is to be appointed by the Minister, after consultation by the Minister with the Minister of Finance and the Board and with the approval of the Governor in Council, for a term of not more than four years that will ensure, to the extent possible, the expiry in any one year of the terms of office of not more than one half of the directors.
No more than two of the directors appointed under subsection (1) may be appointed from among persons who occupy a position in the federal public administration.

The chairperson is to be appointed by the Minister, after consultation by the Minister with the Minister of Finance and the Board and with the approval of the Governor in Council, for a term that the Governor in Council considers appropriate.
A person is not eligible to be appointed or to continue as the chairperson if they occupy a position in the federal public administration.
The chairperson may, with the authorization of the Board, continue in office after the expiration of their term of office until the earlier of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the 180th day after the day on which their term of office ends, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the day on which they are reappointed or their successor is appointed.

Subsection.Text: If the chairperson is absent or unable to act or the office of chairperson is vacant, the Board may authorize one of the other directors to act as chairperson, but that person is not authorized to act as chairperson for a period of more than 180 days without the approval of the Governor in Council.

The chairperson and each of the other directors holds office during pleasure and may be removed by the Minister after consultation by the Minister with the Minister of Finance and the Board and with the approval of the Governor in Council.

The chief executive officer of the Corporation is to be appointed by the Minister, after consultation by the Minister with the Minister of Finance, from among persons nominated by the Board, for a term that the Minister considers appropriate.
A person is not entitled to hold the offices of director and chief executive officer at the same time.
The chief executive officer is eligible to be reappointed at the end of their term of office.
The chief executive officer may, with the authorization of the Board, continue in office after the expiration of their term of office until the earlier of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the 180th day after the day on which their term of office ends, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the day on which they are reappointed or their successor is appointed.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Absence or incapacity — chief executive officer
Subsection.Text: If the chief executive officer is absent or unable to act or the office of chief executive officer is vacant, the Board may authorize an officer or employee of the Corporation to act as chief executive officer, but that person is not authorized to act as chief executive officer for a period of more than 180 days unless the Minister, after consultation by that Minister with the Minister of Finance, approves a longer period.
The chief executive officer holds office during pleasure and may be removed by the Minister after consultation by the Minister with the Minister of Finance and the Board.

Section.MarginalNote: Attendance of chief executive officer at meetings
Section.Text: Subject to any by-law of the Board, the chief executive officer may attend meetings of the Board or any committee of the Board.

The directors, chief executive officer and employees of the Corporation are deemed to be employees for the purposes of the Government Employees Compensation Act and to be employed in the federal public administration for the purposes of any regulations made under section 9 of the Aeronautics Act.

Subsection.Text: The Board must establish guidelines respecting conflicts of interest and confidential information.
The Board must, after establishing or modifying the guidelines, provide a copy of the guidelines to the Minister.
The Minister may, after consulting the Board, modify the guidelines respecting conflicts of interest as they apply to the chief executive officer.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Sections 116 and 117 of Financial Administration Act
Subsection.Text: The guidelines respecting conflicts of interest must be consistent with sections 116 and 117 of the Financial Administration Act.
The Board must establish an evaluation committee that is responsible for advising the Corporation on matters relating to the provision of financial support by the Corporation.
Directors are not eligible to be members of the committee.

The Board may make by-laws respecting the committee, including respecting the matters relating to the provision of financial support on which it is to advise the Corporation and respecting the expertise of its members.

Section.Text: A directive must not be given to the Corporation under subsection 89(1) of the Financial Administration Act requiring the Corporation to provide financial support to a specific entity or a group of specific entities or requiring the Corporation to provide advice to a specific entity or a group of specific entities.

Section.MarginalNote: Disclosure of information to federal institutions

Subsection.Text: The Corporation may disclose any information in its possession to a federal institution if that disclosure is related to the carrying out of the Corporation’s purpose.
A federal institution may disclose any information in its possession to the Corporation if that disclosure is related to the carrying out of the Corporation’s purpose.

Section.MarginalNote: Payments out of Consolidated Revenue Fund

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2), the Minister must pay out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund the following amounts, or any greater amounts specified in an appropriation Act, to the Corporation:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for the financial year ending March 31, 2024, $198,000,000;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for the financial year ending March 31, 2025, $775,000,000;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: for the financial year ending March 31, 2026, $800,000,000;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: for the financial year ending March 31, 2027, $800,000,000; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: for each subsequent financial year, $525,000,000.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, with the approval of the Minister of Finance, withhold all or part of the payment for a financial year.
The Minister must consult the Board before withholding all or part of a payment.

If the Minister withholds a payment, the Minister must cause a report setting out the reasons for withholding the payment to be tabled in each House of Parliament on any of the first 15 days on which that House is sitting after the payment has been withheld.

Within three months after the end of each financial year, the Corporation must pay to the Receiver General the money of the Corporation that was in excess of $66,000,000 at the end of that financial year.
Sections 130 to 130.2 of the Financial Administration Act do not apply in respect of the Corporation.

Section.Text: The financial year of the Corporation is the period of 12 months beginning on April 1 and ending on the next March 31.
The following information must be included in the quarterly financial report that the Corporation is required to cause to be prepared under subsection 131.1(1) of the Financial Administration Act:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the name of each entity with which the Corporation entered into an agreement or arrangement in that quarter to provide financial support; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of the financial support provided for in each agreement or arrangement.

Section.Text: The following information must be included in the annual report that the Corporation is required to submit under subsection 150(1) of the Financial Administration Act:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: information relating to each program that it developed, delivered or administered under paragraph 10(e) in the financial year;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the name of each entity to which it provided financial support of $100,000 or more in that year and the amount of that financial support; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the number of entities to which it provided financial support of less than $100,000 in that year and the total amount of the financial support provided to those entities.

Section.Text: The following definitions apply in sections 26 to 35.

Definition.Text: Director has the same meaning as in subsection 2(1) of the Canada Business Corporations Act. (directeur)
former Corporation means the Canada Innovation Corporation incorporated under the Canada Business Corporations Act. (ancienne Corporation)
new Corporation means the Canada Innovation Corporation continued under section 5. (nouvelle Corporation)

Section.MarginalNote: Copy of order provided to Director

Subsection.Text: The Minister must provide the Director with a copy of the order made under subsection 241(1) of the Budget Implementation Act, 2023, No. 1.

For the purposes of the Canada Business Corporations Act, the copy of the order is deemed to be a notice referred to in subsection 188(7) of that Act, satisfactory to the Director, that the former Corporation has been continued under one of the Acts referred to in subsection 188(2) of that Act.

Section.Text: All the shares of the former Corporation are transferred to the Minister to hold in trust for His Majesty in Right of Canada.

During the period beginning on the day on which this section comes into force and ending on the day on which at least four other directors are in office, the chairperson comprises the Board and may exercise all the powers of the Board.
For greater certainty,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subject to subsections 12(7) and 13(6), the chairperson, directors and chief executive officer of the former Corporation who hold office immediately before the day on which this section comes into force continue to hold office as the chairperson, directors and chief executive officer, respectively, of the new Corporation for the remainder of the term for which they were appointed;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the property and rights of the former Corporation continue to be the property and rights of the new Corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the new Corporation continues to be liable for the obligations of the former Corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: unless the context otherwise requires, every reference to the former Corporation in any contract, instrument or act or other document executed or signed by the former Corporation is to be read as a reference to the new Corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: an existing cause of action, claim or liability to prosecution relating to the former Corporation is unaffected;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a civil, criminal or administrative action or proceeding pending by or against the former Corporation may be continued by or against the new Corporation; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: a conviction against, or ruling, order or judgment in favour of or against, the former Corporation may be enforced by or against the new Corporation.

Subsection.Text: Any person who, immediately before the day on which this section comes into force, occupied a position within the Industrial Research Assistance Program of the National Research Council of Canada, beginning on that day, occupies their position within the new Corporation.

Nothing in subsection (1) or in any regulations made under section 35 is to be construed as affecting the status of such a person except that, beginning on the day on which this section comes into force, the person occupies their position within the new Corporation.
For greater certainty, a person’s status includes whether or not they occupy a managerial or confidential position.

Section.Text: If this section comes into force before March 31, 2024, any money that is appropriated by an Act of Parliament, for the financial year in which this section comes into force, to defray any expenditure of the National Research Council of Canada that is related to the Industrial Research Assistance Program and that, on the day on which this section comes into force, is unexpended is deemed, on that day, to be an amount appropriated to defray any expenditure of the new Corporation. The Minister must pay that amount to the new Corporation.

Section.MarginalNote: Transfer of rights, property and obligations
Section.Text: All rights and property of the National Research Council of Canada that are related to the Industrial Research Assistance Program and of His Majesty in right of Canada that are under the administration and control of the National Research Council of Canada and are related to the Industrial Research Assistance Program and all obligations of the National Research Council of Canada that are related to the Industrial Research Assistance Program are transferred to the new Corporation.

Section.MarginalNote: Transfer of powers, duties and functions
Section.Text: If a power, duty or function is, under any contract, lease, licence or other document, vested in or exercisable by a person who, immediately before the day on which this section comes into force, occupied a position within the Industrial Research Assistance Program of the National Research Council of Canada, the power, duty or function is vested in and is to be exercised by the new Corporation.

Section.MarginalNote: Information about Industrial Research Assistance Program
Section.Text: The National Research Council of Canada may disclose any information that is under its control and that is related to the Industrial Research Assistance Program to the new Corporation.

The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting the transfer of the Industrial Research Assistance Program of the National Research Council of Canada to the new Corporation, including regulations respecting the manner in which rights, property and obligations are to be transferred under section 32 and any restrictions on those transfers.
Part I of Schedule III to the Financial Administration Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:
Part I of Schedule I to the Public Service Superannuation Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: Chief executive officer and employees of the Canada Innovation Corporation
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Premier dirigeant et personnel de la Corporation d’innovation du Canada

Subsection.Text: Sections 1 to 29, 34 and 35 of the Canada Innovation Corporation Act, as enacted by section 238 of this Act, and sections 239 and 240 of this Act come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Sections 30 to 33 of the Canada Innovation Corporation Act, as enacted by section 238 of this Act, come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

Heading.Label: DIVISION 8
Heading.TitleText: Federal-Provincial Fiscal Arrangements Act (Canada Health Transfer)

Section.Text: The Federal-Provincial Fiscal Arrangements Act is amended by adding the following after section 24.73:
The Minister may pay an additional cash payment equal to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for Ontario, $776,262,000;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: for Nova Scotia, $52,306,000;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: for New Brunswick, $41,674,000;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: for Manitoba, $72,450,000;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: for British Columbia, $273,238,000;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: for Prince Edward Island, $8,759,000;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: for Saskatchewan, $61,385,000;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: for Alberta, $233,120,000;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: for Newfoundland and Labrador, $27,051,000;

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: for Yukon, $2,252,000;

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: for the Northwest Territories, $2,348,000; and

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: for Nunavut, $2,088,000.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (2) to (4) apply if Bill C-46, introduced in the 1st Session of the 44th Parliament and entitled the Cost of Living Relief Act, No. 3 (in this section referred to as the “other Act”), receives royal assent.
If section 2 of the other Act comes into force before section 242 of this Act, then that section 242 is deemed never to have come into force and is repealed.
If section 242 of this Act comes into force before section 2 of the other Act, that section 2 is deemed never to have come into force and is repealed.
If section 2 of the other Act comes into force on the same day as section 242 of this Act, then that section 242 is deemed never to have come into force and is repealed.

Heading.Label: DIVISION 9
Heading.TitleText: Federal-Provincial Fiscal Arrangements Act (Equalization and Territorial Financing Renewal and Other Amendments)

Section.Text: Section 3 of the Federal-Provincial Fiscal Arrangements Act is replaced by the following:

Subject to the other provisions of this Act, there may be paid to a province a fiscal equalization payment not exceeding the amounts determined under this Part for each fiscal year in the period beginning on April 1, 2007 and ending on March 31, 2029.

Subsection.Text: The definition revenue to be equalized in subsection 3.5(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: revenue to be equalized means, in respect of a revenue source for a province for a fiscal year,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the revenue, as determined by the Minister, derived by that province for that fiscal year from that revenue source; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a revenue source other than a revenue source referred to in paragraph (e) of the definition revenue source, the product obtained by multiplying the sum of miscellaneous revenues derived by that province for the fiscal year by the quotient obtained by dividing the sum of revenues from that revenue source derived by all provinces for that fiscal year by the sum of all revenues derived by all provinces from the revenue sources referred to in paragraphs (a) to (d) of the definition revenue source for that fiscal year.

ContinuedDefinition.Text: It may be defined more particularly by the regulations. (revenu sujet à péréquation)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (d) of the definition revenue source in subsection 3.5(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: revenues derived from property taxes; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 3.5(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of this Part, the revenue derived by each municipality, board, commission or other local authority in a province that has power to derive those revenues for the financial year of each such local authority ending in that fiscal year is both deemed to be revenue derived by that province and

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of local government property taxes, property taxes within the meaning of paragraph (d) of the definition revenue source in subsection (1); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of other miscellaneous local government taxes and revenues, included in miscellaneous revenues for the purpose of the definition revenue to be equalized in subsection (1).

Subsection.Text: The definition revenue to be equalized in subsection 4(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: revenue to be equalized, in respect of a revenue source for a territory for a fiscal year, means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the revenue, as determined by the Minister, derived by that territory for that fiscal year from that revenue source; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a revenue source referred to in paragraphs (a), (b), (h) and (i) of the definition revenue source, the product obtained by multiplying the sum of miscellaneous revenues derived by that territory for the fiscal year by the quotient obtained by dividing the sum of revenues from that revenue source derived by all territories for that fiscal year by the sum of all revenues derived by all territories from the revenue sources referred to in paragraphs (a), (b), (h) and (i) of the definition revenue source for the fiscal year.

ContinuedDefinition.Text: It may be defined more particularly by the regulations. (revenu sujet à péréquation)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (h) of the definition revenue source in subsection 4(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: revenues derived from property taxes; and

Section.Text: Subsection 4.1(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to the other provisions of this Act, there may be paid to a territory a territorial formula financing payment not exceeding the amounts determined under this Part for each fiscal year in the period beginning on April 1, 2014 and ending on March 31, 2029.

Section.Text: Subsection 6(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of determining the amount under subsection (1), the Minister may adjust, in the prescribed manner, the natural resource revenue and the non-natural resource revenue of a province for the fiscal year

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to offset the amount, as determined by the Minister, of any change in either of those revenues for the fiscal year resulting from changes made by the province in the rates or structure of provincial taxes or of other means of raising the revenue of the province from the rates or structures in effect in the preceding fiscal year; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to account for the absence of indexation in a provincial personal income tax system.

Section.Text: Section 40 of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a.4):

Paragraph.Label: (a.5)
Paragraph.Text: providing for the provincial and territorial revenues that constitute miscellaneous revenues for the purposes of the definition revenue to be equalized in subsections 3.5(1) and 4(1);

Paragraph.Label: (a.6)
Paragraph.Text: respecting what constitutes the absence of indexation in a provincial personal income tax system for the purpose of paragraph 6(2)(b);

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 41:
The Minister shall publish the details of all amounts authorized to be paid under this Act on the website of the Department of Finance as soon as feasible after the payment of such amounts.
The provisions of the Federal-Provincial Fiscal Arrangements Act enacted by sections 243 to 246 of this Act apply to fiscal years that begin after March 31, 2024.
The provisions of the Federal-Provincial Fiscal Arrangements Act enacted by section 247 of this Act apply to fiscal years that begin after March 31, 2021.

The provisions of Parts I and I.1 of the Federal-Provincial Fiscal Arrangements Act — and of any regulations made under that Act — as they read immediately before the day on which this Act receives royal assent continue to apply to fiscal years that end before April 1, 2024.
The heading before section 2 of the French version of the Special Economic Measures Act is replaced by the following:
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 2:

Subsection.Text: If a person controls an entity other than a foreign state, any property that is owned — or that is held or controlled, directly or indirectly — by the entity is deemed to be owned by that person.
For the purposes of subsection (1), a person controls an entity, directly or indirectly, if any of the following criteria are met:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person holds, directly or indirectly, 50% or more of the shares or ownership interests in the entity or 50% or more of the voting rights in the entity;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the person is able, directly or indirectly, to change the composition or powers of the entity’s board of directors; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: it is reasonable to conclude, having regard to all the circumstances, that the person is able, directly or indirectly and through any means, to direct the entity’s activities.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 4(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: by order, cause to be seized or restrained in the manner set out in the order any property situated in Canada that is owned — or that is held or controlled, directly or indirectly — by a foreign state or a person who is identified in an order or regulation made under paragraph (1)(a).

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 4(2)(a) to (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any dealing by any person in Canada or Canadian outside Canada in any property, wherever situated, that is owned — or that is held or controlled, directly or indirectly — by that foreign state, any person in that foreign state, a national of that foreign state who does not ordinarily reside in Canada or a person outside Canada who is not Canadian;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the exportation, sale, supply or shipment by any person in Canada or Canadian outside Canada of any goods wherever situated to that foreign state, any person in that foreign state or a person outside Canada who is not Canadian, or any other dealing by any person in Canada or Canadian outside Canada in any goods wherever situated destined for that foreign state, any person in that foreign state or a person outside Canada who is not Canadian;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the transfer, provision or communication by any person in Canada or Canadian outside Canada of any technical data to that foreign state, any person in that foreign state or a person outside Canada who is not Canadian;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 4(2)(e) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the provision or acquisition by any person in Canada or Canadian outside Canada of financial services or any other services to, from or for the benefit of or on the direction or order of that foreign state, any person in that foreign state or a person outside Canada who is not Canadian;

Paragraph.Label: (e.1)
Paragraph.Text: the transfer or provision by any person in Canada or Canadian outside Canada of property other than goods to that foreign state, any person in that foreign state, a national of that foreign state who does not ordinarily reside in Canada or a person outside Canada who is not Canadian;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 4(2)(h) and (i) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the docking in or passage through Canada by ships registered in that foreign state or used, leased or chartered, in whole or in part, by or on behalf of or for the benefit of that foreign state, any person in that foreign state or a person outside Canada who is not Canadian; and

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: the landing in or flight over Canada by aircraft registered in that foreign state or used, leased or chartered, in whole or in part, by or on behalf of or for the benefit of that foreign state, any person in that foreign state or a person outside Canada who is not Canadian.

Section.Text: Paragraph 5.2(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person is the foreign state identified in the order or a person identified in the order; or

Section.Text: Subsection 5.4(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Any person — other than a person referred to in paragraph 5.2(a) — who claims an interest in or right to property that is forfeited to Her Majesty under subsection (1) may, within 30 days after the day on which the property is forfeited, apply in writing to a judge for an order declaring that their interest or right is not affected by the forfeiture, declaring the nature and extent of the interest or right and directing the Minister to pay to the person an amount equal to the value of their interest or right.

Section.Text: Section 6.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (d):

Paragraph.Label: (d.1)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister of Transport;

Paragraph.Label: (d.2)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister of National Revenue;

Paragraph.Label: (d.3)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister of Justice and Attorney General of Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (d.4)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister of Citizenship and Immigration;

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 6.2:

The Minister may disclose to the Financial Transactions and Reports Analysis Centre of Canada any information that is relevant to the making, administration or enforcement of an order or regulation referred to in subsection 4(1).

Heading.TitleText: Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act

Section.Text: Subsection 55(3) of the Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (f.1) and by adding the following after paragraph (g):

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister of Foreign Affairs or a Minister designated under subsection 6(2) of the Special Economic Measures Act, if the Centre also determines that the information is relevant to the making, administration or enforcement of an order or regulation referred to in subsection 4(1) of that Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister of Foreign Affairs or a Minister designated under subsection 2.1(2) of the Justice for Victims of Corrupt Foreign Officials Act (Sergei Magnitsky Law), if the Centre also determines that the information is relevant to the making, administration or enforcement of an order or regulation referred to in subsection 4(1) of that Act.

Heading.TitleText: Justice for Victims of Corrupt Foreign Officials Act (Sergei Magnitsky Law)

Section.Text: The heading before section 2 of the French version of the Justice for Victims of Corrupt Foreign Officials Act (Sergei Magnitsky Law) is replaced by the following:
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 2:

Subsection.Text: If a foreign national controls an entity other than a foreign state, any property that is owned — or that is held or controlled, directly or indirectly — by the entity is deemed to be owned by that foreign national.
For the purposes of subsection (1), a foreign national controls an entity, directly or indirectly, if any of the following criteria are met:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the foreign national holds, directly or indirectly, 50% or more of the shares or ownership interests in the entity or 50% or more of the voting rights in the entity;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the foreign national is able, directly or indirectly, to change the composition or powers of the entity’s board of directors; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: it is reasonable to conclude, having regard to all the circumstances, that the foreign national is able, directly or indirectly and through any means, to direct the entity’s activities.

Section.Text: Paragraph 4(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: by order, cause to be seized or restrained in the manner set out in the order any property situated in Canada that is owned — or that is held or controlled, directly or indirectly — by a foreign national who is identified in an order or regulation made under paragraph (1)(a).

Section.Text: Section 7.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (d):

Paragraph.Label: (d.1)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister of Transport;

Paragraph.Label: (d.2)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister of National Revenue;

Paragraph.Label: (d.3)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister of Justice and Attorney General of Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (d.4)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister of Citizenship and Immigration;

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 7.2:

The Minister may disclose to the Financial Transactions and Reports Analysis Centre of Canada any information that is relevant to the making, administration or enforcement of an order or regulation referred to in subsection 4(1).

Heading.Label: DIVISION 11
Heading.TitleText: Privileges and Immunities (North Atlantic Treaty Organisation) Act

Section.Text: The long title of the Privileges and Immunities (North Atlantic Treaty Organisation) Act is replaced by the following:

AmendedText.LongTitle: An Act to provide for privileges and immunities in respect of the North Atlantic Treaty Organisation and any international military Headquarters or organisation established under that Treaty

Section.Text: Sections 2 and 3 of the Act are replaced by the following:
The following definitions apply in this Act.

Definition.Text: Ottawa Agreement means the Agreement on the status of the North Atlantic Treaty Organisation, National Representatives and International Staff, done at Ottawa on September 20, 1951. (Convention d’Ottawa)
Paris Protocol means the Protocol on the Status of International Military Headquarters set up pursuant to the North Atlantic Treaty, done at Paris on August 28, 1952. (Protocole de Paris)

Section.Text: The Ottawa Agreement set out in Schedule 1 and the Paris Protocol set out in Schedule 2 are approved.

The Governor in Council may make any orders that the Governor in Council considers necessary for the purpose of carrying out the obligations and exercising the rights of Canada under the Ottawa Agreement, the Paris Protocol and any agreement under Article 25 of that Agreement or paragraph 2 of Article 16 of that Protocol.

The Governor in Council may make any orders that the Governor in Council considers necessary to enable any international military Headquarters or organization established under the North Atlantic Treaty to exercise its powers or perform its functions in Canada, including any orders that impose obligations and confer rights, privileges and immunities comparable to those under the Paris Protocol.

A certificate issued by or under the authority of the Minister of Foreign Affairs and containing any statement of fact relevant to any of the following questions is admissible in evidence in any action or proceeding as proof of the fact stated in the certificate without proof of the signature or official character of the person appearing to have signed the certificate:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: whether the North Atlantic Treaty Organization, any of its subsidiary bodies or any international military Headquarters or organization is the subject of an order made under section 4 or 5;
