Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: whether the North Atlantic Treaty Organization, any of its subsidiary bodies or any international military Headquarters or organization has privileges or immunities provided by an order made under section 4 or 5;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: whether any person has privileges or immunities provided by an order made under section 4 or 5.

Section.Text: The schedule to the Act is numbered as Schedule 1.
Schedule 1 to the Act is amended by replacing the reference after the heading “SCHEDULE 1” with the following:
The Act is amended by adding, after Schedule 1, the Schedule 2 set out in the schedule to this Act.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (e) of the definition fee in subsection 2(1) of the Service Fees Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the provision of a regulatory process. (frais)

Subsection.Text: Section 2 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):
This Act does not apply to a fee if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the fee is fixed under the Access to Information Act or the Privacy Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the fee is paid only by or on behalf of a minister or federal entity.

Subsection.Text: Section 3 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: Sections 4 to 7 do not apply to a fee if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the fee is fixed by contract;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the person or body that fixes the fee does so by fixing a manner for determining the amount of the fee over which that person or body has no control, such as an auction or a method that is based on the market rate; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the fee is referred to in paragraph (d) of the definition fee.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 3(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the President of the Treasury Board approves that those sections do not apply and the person or body that fixes the fee does so by fixing a manner for determining the amount of the fee over which that person or body has no control, such as an auction or a method that is based on the market rate; or

Subsection.Text: Section 9 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: Sections 10 to 15 do not apply to a fee if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the fee is fixed by contract;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the person or body that fixes the fee does so by fixing a manner for determining the amount of the fee over which that person or body has no control, such as an auction or a method that is based on the market rate;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the fee is fixed by a regulation, as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Statutory Instruments Act, that is published in Part I of the Canada Gazette before it is made; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: an Act of Parliament other than this Act requires consultation before the fee is fixed.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 9(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the President of the Treasury Board approves that those sections do not apply and the person or body that fixes the fee does so by fixing a manner for determining the amount of the fee over which that person or body has no control, such as an auction or a method that is based on the market rate;

Subsection.Text: The portion of section 16 of the English version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: Sections 17 and 18 do not apply to a fee if

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 16(b) to (e) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the person or body that fixes the fee does so by fixing a manner for determining the amount of the fee over which that person or body has no control, such as an auction or a method that is based on the market rate; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the fee is adjusted periodically by operation of an Act of Parliament other than this Act or by operation of an instrument made under such an Act.

Subsection.Text: Section 16 of the Act is replaced by the following:
Sections 17 and 18 do not apply to a fee if the fee is fixed by contract.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Approval by President of Treasury Board
Subsection.Text: Sections 17 and 18 do not apply to a fee if the President of the Treasury Board approves that those sections do not apply and

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person or body that fixes the fee does so by fixing a manner for determining the amount of the fee over which that person or body has no control, such as an auction or a method that is based on the market rate;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the fee is fixed in a manner that takes inflation into account; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the fee is adjusted periodically by operation of an Act of Parliament other than this Act or by operation of an instrument made under such an Act.

Section.Text: Subsection 17(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A fee is not adjusted by operation of subsection (1) in a fiscal year if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the fee is fixed in that fiscal year before the adjustment date; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the percentage change described in subsection (1) for that fiscal year is less than 1%.

Subsection.Text: If paragraph (2)(b) applies with respect to a fiscal year, the percentage change referred to in that paragraph is carried forward to the subsequent fiscal year. If the combined percentage change is less than 1%, the fee is not adjusted for that subsequent fiscal year and the combined percentage change is carried forward to the next subsequent fiscal year.
The responsible authority may, in accordance with Treasury Board policies or directives, round down a fee after an adjustment under subsection (1) in a given fiscal year.
Despite subsection (1), the responsible authority may change the anniversary date selected in respect of a fee in accordance with Treasury Board policies or directives.

Section.Text: Section 19 of the Act is repealed.

Subsection.Text: Section 21 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: The President of the Treasury Board must, no later than March 31 of a fiscal year in which any report referred to in subsection 20(1) is tabled, make a report accessible to the public that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: consolidates the information set out in the tabled reports; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: lists the amendments to the regulations made by the President of the Treasury Board under section 22 in that fiscal year.

Subsection.Text: Section 21 of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (a), by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: lists the approvals given by the President of the Treasury Board in that fiscal year under this Act.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 22(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: listing the fees that are considered to be low-materiality fees or the fees that are not considered to be low-materiality fees;

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: setting out criteria for determining whether fees are low-materiality fees and when low-materiality fees cease to be low-materiality fees;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 22(2) of the Act is amended by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after that paragraph:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: establishing the factors that the President of the Treasury Board must take into account while exercising the power referred to in subsection (3).

Subsection.Text: Section 22 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Amending regulations — President of the Treasury Board
Subsection.Text: The President of the Treasury Board may, by regulation, amend regulations made under paragraph (2)(a), taking into account any factors established under paragraph (2)(c).

Heading.TitleText: Consequential Amendment to the Canadian Food Inspection Agency Act

Section.Text: Section 25.1 of the Canadian Food Inspection Agency Act is renumbered as subsection 25.1(1) and is amended by adding the following:

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection 22(1) of the Service Fees Act, sections 16 to 18 of that Act apply to a low-materiality fee within the meaning of that Act that is fixed under section 24 for a service or the use of a facility provided by the Agency under the Safe Food for Canadians Act or that is fixed under section 25 in respect of products, rights and privileges provided by the Agency under the Safe Food for Canadians Act.

Section.Text: Subsections 271(2), 272(2), 273(3) and 276(2) come into force on April 1, 2024.
Section 92 of the Canada Pension Plan is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Information provided by Minister of National Revenue
Subsection.Text: Despite any other Act or law, the Minister of National Revenue or any person that he or she designates may make available to the Minister a report providing information that is necessary for the purpose of policy analysis, research or evaluation related to the administration of this Act.

Heading.Label: DIVISION 14
Heading.TitleText: Department of Employment and Social Development Act

Section.Text: The Department of Employment and Social Development Act is amended by adding the following after section 8:

The Minister may collect and use a person’s Social Insurance Number to verify their identity for the purposes of the administration or enforcement of any Act, program or activity in respect of which the administration or enforcement is the responsibility of the Minister.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 206.5(2) to (4) of the Canada Labour Code are replaced by the following:

Every employee is entitled to and shall be granted a leave of absence from employment of up to 156 weeks if the employee is the parent of a child who has died and it is probable, considering the circumstances, that the child died as a result of a crime.

Every employee is entitled to and shall be granted a leave of absence from employment of up to 156 weeks if the employee is the parent of a child who has disappeared and it is probable, considering the circumstances, that the child disappeared as a result of a crime.
An employee is not entitled to a leave of absence if they are charged with the crime.
Paragraph 206.5(5)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: ends 156 weeks after the day on which the death or disappearance, as the case may be, occurs.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 206.5(6)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: on the 14th day after the day on which the child is found, if the child is found during the 156-week period, but no later than the end of the 156-week period; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: 156 weeks after the day on which the disappearance occurs if subsection (2) applies to the child.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 206.5(8) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The aggregate amount of leave that may be taken by employees under this section in respect of the same death or disappearance of a child — or the same children who die or disappear as a result of the same event — must not exceed 156 weeks.

Section.Text: Paragraph 209.4(h) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing cases, other than the one set out in subsection 206.5(4), in which an employee is not entitled to a leave of absence and cases in which an employee is entitled to a leave of absence even if they are charged with the crime;

Heading.Label: DIVISION 16
Heading.TitleText: Immigration and Refugee Protection Act (Claims for Refugee Protection)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 99(3) of the Immigration and Refugee Protection Act is replaced by the following:
A claim for refugee protection made by a person inside Canada must be made in person to an officer, must not be made by a person who is subject to a removal order, and is governed by this Part.
Subsection 99(3.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Claim made inside Canada — not at port of entry
Subsection.Text: A person inside Canada who makes a claim for refugee protection or who, before making a claim, submits any document or provides information with a view to making a claim, other than at a port of entry must provide the officer, within the time limits provided for in the regulations, with the documents and information, including in respect of the basis for the claim, required by the rules of the Board or specified by the Minister. The documents and information are to be provided in accordance with those rules and in the form and manner specified by the Minister.

Section.Text: Subsection 284(2) comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

Heading.Label: DIVISION 17
Heading.TitleText: Immigration and Refugee Protection Act (Sponsorship Applications)

Section.Text: Section 87.3 of the Immigration and Refugee Protection Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, this section applies to a sponsorship application made under subsection 13(1) to sponsor a person referred to in subsection 99(2).

Heading.Label: DIVISION 18
Heading.TitleText: College of Immigration and Citizenship Consultants Act

Section.Text: Section 4 of the College of Immigration and Citizenship Consultants Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a):

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: establishing and providing training and development programs for licensees;

Section.Text: Subsection 15(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The College must submit to the Minister, within 120 days after the end of each of its fiscal years, a report on the College’s activities during the preceding fiscal year.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 39:

Subsection.Text: The College may file in the Federal Court a certified copy of a decision made under section 38.
On the certified copy being filed, the decision becomes and may be enforced as an order of the Federal Court.

Section.Text: Section 56 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Responsibility for damages — directors and others
Section.Text: No action or other proceeding for damages lies or may be instituted against any of the following persons for anything done or omitted to be done in good faith in the exercise or purported exercise of any power, or in the performance or purported performance of any duty or function, conferred on that person under the Act:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a current or former director of the Board;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a current or former member of a committee of the College;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the Registrar or a former Registrar;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a current or former investigator;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a current or former officer, employee, agent or mandatary of the College; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a person who is or has been engaged by the College.

Section.Text: Paragraph 57(2)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: refer the complaint to a process of dispute resolution, on any conditions specified by the Complaints Committee, if the licensee consents.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 69(3) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

If the Discipline Committee determines that the licensee has committed professional misconduct or was incompetent, the Committee may, in its decision, take or require one or more of the following actions as well as any other action set out in the regulations:
Subsection 69(3) of the Act is amended by adding “or” at the end of paragraph (c), by striking out “or” at the end of paragraph (d) and by repealing paragraph (e).

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 70:

Subsection.Text: The College may file in the Federal Court a certified copy of a decision made under subsection 69(3).
On the certified copy being filed, the decision becomes and may be enforced as an order of the Federal Court.

Section.Text: Section 71 of the Act is renumbered as subsection 71(1) and is amended by adding the following:

Subsection.Text: However, if the College makes an application for judicial review of a decision of its committees, the committee is the respondent.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 73:

Subsection.Text: If a licensee is not able to perform their activities as an immigration and citizenship consultant for any prescribed reason, the College may, in order to carry out its purpose, including to ensure the continuation of services to a licensee’s client, apply, without notice, to a court of competent jurisdiction, including the Federal Court, for an order authorizing the College or a person appointed by the court to administer any property in relation to those activities that is or should be in the licensee’s possession or under their control.

If the court concludes that it is necessary to ensure the continuation of services to the licensee’s clients or to otherwise permit the College to carry out its purpose, it may, for the purposes of subsection (1),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: authorize the College or person appointed by the court to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: enter a place, including a vehicle, in which the College or person has reasonable grounds to believe property referred to in subsection (1) is located,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: examine anything in the place,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: open or order any person to open any container or package found in the place,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: require the owner or person in charge of the place and any person in the place to give all assistance that is reasonably required to enable the College or person appointed by the court to execute the order and to provide any property referred to in subsection (1) or any information, and access to any data, that are reasonably required for that purpose, and

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: seize any document or other thing related to a client’s files and transfer it to the client or a licensee or person referred to in paragraph 21.1(2)(a) or (b) or subsection 21.1(3) or (4) of the Citizenship Act or paragraph 91(2)(a) or (b) or subsection 91(3) or (4) of the Immigration and Refugee Protection Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: order that any property referred to in subsection (1) is not to be dealt with by any person without leave of the court or is to be held in trust by the College or person appointed by the court;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: require the licensee to account to the College or person appointed by the court for any property referred to in subsection (1);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: provide for the compensation of the College or person appointed by the court for administering the licensee’s property and the reimbursement of any expenses the College or person incurs in doing so; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: provide for any other matter that it considers appropriate in the circumstances, including imposing conditions on the manner in which the College or person appointed by the court is to execute the order.

Subsection.Text: The court may also authorize the College or person appointed by the court to seize and detain any property referred to in subsection (1) found in the place and transfer it to any person for the purpose of carrying on the licensee’s business operations or winding up their practice.
For greater certainty, the College has jurisdiction to apply for an order referred to in subsection (1) in respect of a former licensee.

Section.Text: The College is not permitted to make an application under subsection 73.1(1) after the sixth anniversary of the day on which a licensee ceases to be a licensee.
Sections 52 and 53 apply in respect of an order issued under subsection 73.1(1) with any adaptations that may be necessary.

It is prohibited to obstruct or hinder, or make a false or misleading statement either orally or in writing, to the College or person appointed by the Court when the College or person is executing an order issued under subsection 73.1(1).

Subsection.Text: The College may enter into an agreement or arrangement with any entity, including a government institution, as defined in section 3 of the Access to Information Act, or an institution of a government of a province or foreign state, for the sharing of information for the purpose of assisting in the administration and enforcement of this Act, or any other Act of Parliament or of a province or any other law, as it relates to immigration and citizenship consultants.
An agreement or arrangement entered into under subsection (1) is subject to the by-laws and regulations made under this Act.

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 79(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every person who contravenes section 55, 70 or 73.4 or an order made under paragraph 66(a) is guilty of an offence and liable

Section.Text: Subsection 81(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Regulations made under paragraphs (1)(c) to (f), (h) to (j), (l) to (p), (r), (s), (u) and (x) may authorize the College to make by-laws with respect to all or part of the subject matter of the regulations and, for greater certainty, those by-laws are regulations for the purposes of the Statutory Instruments Act.

Section.Text: Subsection 27(3) of the Citizenship Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Any regulation made under paragraph (1)(k.6) must provide that a person who is the subject of a notice of violation has the right to request, from a person appointed under subsection (4), a review of the notice or of the penalty imposed.

Section.Text: Subsection 91.1(2) of the Immigration and Refugee Protection Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Any regulation made under paragraph (1)(a) must provide that a person who is the subject of a notice of violation has the right to request, from a person appointed under subsection (3), a review of the notice or of the penalty imposed.

Section.Text: The Citizenship Act is amended by adding the following after section 2:
The Minister may, for the purpose of administering and enforcing this Act, collect, use, verify, retain and disclose biometric information in accordance with the regulations.

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2) and the regulations, a person who makes an application or request under this Act must provide the biometric information specified by the regulations in accordance with the procedures set out in the regulations for the collection and verification of biometric information.

If the Minister considers that exceptional circumstances warrant it, the Minister may, on the Minister’s own initiative, decide that subsection (1) does not apply in respect of a class of persons and may specify the biometric information that a person who is a member of that class is to provide and the procedure to follow for the collection and verification of that biometric information.

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 12(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Minister shall, on application by a person, determine whether they are a citizen and, if they are,
Subsection 27(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following before paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the application of section 2.2, including regulations respecting

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: restrictions on the persons to whom and on the applications or requests to which that section applies,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the biometric information that is to be provided,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the circumstances in which a person is not required to provide certain biometric information, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the circumstances in which a person is exempt from the application of that section;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 27(1)(k.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (k.1)
Paragraph.Text: providing for the collection, retention, use, verification, disclosure and disposal of information for the purposes of this Act;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 27(1) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (k.9) and by adding the following after that paragraph:

Paragraph.Label: (k.91)
Paragraph.Text: providing the circumstances in which the Minister may require a person, or a person who is a member of a class of persons, who makes an application or request or who provides any document, information or evidence under this Act to do so using any means that are specified by the Minister; and

Section.Text: Subsection 27.2 of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a):

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the procedures for the collection and verification of biometric information;

Paragraph.Label: (a.2)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the processing of collected biometric information, including the creation of biometric templates;

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 27.2:

Subsection.Text: The Minister may make regulations respecting the application of section 28.2 and paragraph 28.3(b), including regulations respecting

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the technology or format to be used, or the standards, specifications or processes to be followed, including for the making or verifying of an electronic signature and the manner in which it is to be used; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the date and time when, and the place where, an electronic version of an application, request, notice, decision, determination or document or any information or evidence is deemed to be sent or received.

Subsection.Text: The regulations made under subsection (1) may include provisions requiring a person who makes an application or request or who provides any document, information or evidence under this Act to do so using electronic means, including an electronic system, and provisions respecting such means, including such a system. The regulations may also include provisions prescribing the circumstances in which the application, request, document, information or evidence may be provided by other means and respecting those other means.
The regulations may also include provisions

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: requiring that payments that are required to be made or evidence of payment that is required to be provided under this Act must be made or provided by means of an electronic system and respecting such a system;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing the circumstances in which such payments may be made or evidence of such payments may be provided by other means and respecting such other means; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the date and time when, and the place where, an electronic payment or evidence of payment is deemed to be sent or received.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 28.1:

Subsection.Text: The Minister may administer and enforce this Act using electronic means.

A citizenship judge and the Registrar of Canadian Citizenship may, in the exercise of their powers and performance of their duties and functions under this Act, use the electronic means that are made available or specified by the Minister.

A person who has been authorized by the Minister to do anything that may be done by the Minister under this Act, may do so using the electronic means that are made available or specified by the Minister.
For greater certainty, an electronic system, including an automated system, may be used to process an application, proceed with an examination or make a decision or determination under this Act.

Section.Text: If any provision of this Act or the regulations requires a signature or any information or evidence to be provided, an application, request, decision or determination to be made, a notice to be given or any other document to be issued or otherwise provided, the electronic version of the signature, information or evidence, application, request, decision or determination, notice or other document is satisfied by its electronic version, if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the electronic version is provided by the electronic means, including an electronic system, that are made available or specified by the Minister; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any other requirements that may be prescribed are met.

Section.Text: For greater certainty, if any provision of this Act or the regulations authorizes the Minister to require that any document, information or evidence be provided, the Minister is not precluded by this Part from requiring that the document, information or evidence, as the case may be, be provided in accordance with that provision.
Section 300, subsections 302(1) and (2) and section 303 come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
The Yukon Act is amended by adding the following after section 55:

Subsection.Text: If the Minister has the administration of public real property that is a “Type II Site” within the meaning of the Yukon Northern Affairs Program Devolution Transfer Agreement made on October 29, 2001, the Minister may take any measures on the public real property that the Minister considers necessary to prevent, counteract, mitigate or remedy any adverse effect on persons, property or the environment.

For the purposes of subsection (1), the Minister may enter any place on the public real property, except a place that is designed to be used and is being used as a permanent or temporary private dwelling-place.

Section.Text: The heading “Interpretation” before section 24 of the Marine Liability Act is replaced by the following:

Heading.Label: DIVISION 1
Heading.TitleText: Convention on Limitation of Liability for Maritime Claims, 1976

Section.Text: The portion of section 24 of the Act before the first definition is replaced by the following:
The following definitions apply in this Division.
The portion of subsection 25(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this Division and Articles 1 to 15 of the Convention,
Subsection 26(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Subject to the other provisions of this Division, Articles 1 to 15 and 18 of the Convention and Articles 8 and 9 of the Protocol have the force of law in Canada.
Subsection 26(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:
This Division does not apply to a claim that is the subject of a reservation made by Canada.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 29(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: $1,500,000 in respect of claims for loss of life or personal injury; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: $750,000 in respect of any other claims.

Section.Text: Subsection 33(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a):

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: permitting that person to reduce the amount that they are required to pay to the fund by up to an amount equal to the sum of any amounts paid to a claimant before the constitution of the fund in respect of a maritime claim for oil pollution damage caused by oil of any kind or in any form other than oil as defined in Article I of the Civil Liability Convention as defined in subsection 47(1);

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 33:

Subsection.Text: In the case of a maritime claim referred to in paragraph 33(1)(a.1), the person constituting the fund must, as soon as feasible, give notice of the fund’s constitution in

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Canada Gazette;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a newspaper in general circulation in the region where the incident occurred; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any other appropriate manner in the communities potentially affected by the incident.

Subsection.Text: Notices given under paragraphs (1)(b) and (c) are to be made accessible to the public for a period of at least 30 days.

The person constituting the fund must, within 15 days after the 30th day of the period referred to in subsection (2), file evidence in the Admiralty Court that the notices have been made accessible to the public for a period of at least 30 days.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Court order in case of non-compliance
Subsection.Text: The Admiralty Court may issue any order that it deems appropriate to remedy the failure of the person to give any of the required public notices or the inadequacy of a notice.

Section.Text: Subsection 34(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If a ship or other property is released under paragraph 2 of Article 13 of the Convention, in any case other than one in which a fund has been constituted in a place described in paragraphs 2(a) to (d) of that Article, the person who obtained the release is deemed to have submitted to the jurisdiction of the court that ordered the release for the purpose of determining the claim.

Section.Text: Paragraph 34.1(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: generally for carrying out the purposes and provisions of this Division.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 34.1:

Heading.Label: DIVISION 2
Heading.TitleText: Limitation of Liability — Air Cushion Vehicles

Section.Text: The following definitions apply in this Division.

Definition.Text: Convention means the Convention on Limitation of Liability for Maritime Claims, 1976, concluded at London on November 19, 1976, as amended by the Protocol, Articles 1 to 15 of which Convention are set out in Part 1 of Schedule 1. (Convention)
owner, in relation to an air cushion vehicle, means the owner, charterer, manager and operator of an air cushion vehicle. (propriétaire)
passenger means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a person carried on board an air cushion vehicle

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: under a contract of passenger carriage, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: to accompany, with the consent of the carrier, a vehicle or live animals which are covered by a contract for the carriage of goods; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a participant in an adventure tourism activity referred to in subsection 37.1(1). (passager)

Definition.Text: Protocol means the Protocol of 1996 to amend the Convention on Limitation of Liability for Maritime Claims, 1976, concluded at London on May 2, 1996. (Protocole)

Subsection.Text: Subject to the other provisions of this Division, Article 1, paragraphs 1(a) to (c), (e) and (f) and paragraph 2 of Article 2, paragraphs (a), (c) and (e) of Article 3, Articles 4 and 5, paragraphs 2 and 3 of Article 6, paragraph 1 of Article 9 and Articles 10 to 14 of the Convention have the force of law in Canada in relation to air cushion vehicles.
For the purposes of this Division, the provisions of the Convention referred to in subsection (1) are adapted as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a reference to “ship” or “vessel” is to be read as a reference to “air cushion vehicle”;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a reference to “shipowner” is to be read as a reference to “owner”;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 2 of Article 1 of the Convention is to be read without reference to the word “seagoing”;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the expression “carriage by sea” in paragraph 1(b) of Article 2 of the Convention is to be read as “carriage by water”;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the first reference to “paragraph 1(a)” in paragraph 2 of Article 6 of the Convention is to be read as reference to “subsection 34.4(1), (2) or (4)” of this Act and the second reference to “paragraph 1(a)” is to be read as a reference to “subsections 34.4(1), (2) and (4) in proportion to the amount of the established claims under each of those subsections”;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a reference to “paragraph 1(b)” in paragraphs 2 and 3 of Article 6 of the Convention is to be read as a reference to “subsection 34.4(5)” of this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the reference to “Article 6” in paragraph 1 of Article 9 of the Convention is to be read as a reference to “section 34.4” of this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the reference to “Articles 6 and 7” in paragraph 1 of Article 11 of the Convention is to be read as a reference to “section 34.4” of this Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: the reference to “the provisions of paragraphs 1, 2 and 3 of Article 6 and of Article 7” in paragraph 1 of Article 12 of the Convention is to be read as a reference to “section 34.4” of this Act.

Subsection.Text: In the event of any inconsistency between sections 34.4 to 34.7 of this Act and the provisions of the Convention referred to in subsection (1), those sections prevail to the extent of the inconsistency.
The maximum liability for claims that arise on any distinct occasion for loss of life or personal injury to passengers of an air cushion vehicle of less than 8 000 kg all up weight is the greater of

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: $300,000 multiplied by

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the number of passengers that the air cushion vehicle is authorized to carry according to any Canadian maritime document required under the Canada Shipping Act, 2001, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the number of passengers on board the air cushion vehicle, if no Canadian maritime document is required under that Act.

Subsection.Text: The maximum liability for claims that arise on any distinct occasion for loss of life or personal injury to persons carried on board an air cushion vehicle of less than 8 000 kg all up weight otherwise than under a contract of passenger carriage is the greater of

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: $300,000 multiplied by

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the number of passengers that the air cushion vehicle is authorized to carry according to any Canadian maritime document required under the Canada Shipping Act, 2001, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the number of persons on board the air cushion vehicle, if no Canadian maritime document is required under that Act.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (2) does not apply in respect of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the master of an air cushion vehicle, a member of an air cushion vehicle’s crew — or any other person employed or engaged in any capacity on the business of an air cushion vehicle — when they are carried on board the air cushion vehicle;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a person carried on board an air cushion vehicle that is not operated for a commercial or public purpose;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a person carried on board an air cushion vehicle in pursuance of the obligation on the master to carry shipwrecked, distressed or other persons or by reason of any circumstances that neither the master nor the owner of the air cushion vehicle could have prevented; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a stowaway, a trespasser or any other person who boards an air cushion vehicle without the consent or knowledge of the master or the owner of the air cushion vehicle.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Other claims — loss of life or personal injury
Subsection.Text: The maximum liability for claims that arise on any distinct occasion for loss of life or personal injury involving an air cushion vehicle, other than claims referred to in subsections (1) and (2), is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: $1,500,000 for an air cushion vehicle of less than 8 000 kg all up weight;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: $5,000,000 for an air cushion vehicle of 8 000 kg or more but less than 13 000 kg all up weight; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: $5,000,000 for an air cushion vehicle of 13 000 kg or more all up weight, plus $40 for each kilogram that exceeds 13 000 kg.

Subsection.Text: The maximum liability for claims that arise on any distinct occasion involving an air cushion vehicle, other than claims referred to in subsections (1), (2) and (4), is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: $750,000 for an air cushion vehicle of less than 8 000 kg all up weight;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: $2,500,000 for an air cushion vehicle of 8 000 kg or more but less than 13 000 kg all up weight; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: $2,500,000 for an air cushion vehicle of 13 000 kg or more all up weight, plus $20 for each kilogram that exceeds 13 000 kg.

Subsection.Text: The Admiralty Court has exclusive jurisdiction with respect to any matter relating to the constitution and distribution of a limitation fund under Articles 11 to 13 of the Convention.

If a claim is made or apprehended against a person in respect of liability that is limited by section 34.4, that person may assert the right to limitation of liability in a defence filed, or by way of action or counterclaim for declaratory relief, in any court of competent jurisdiction in Canada.

If a claim is made or apprehended against a person in respect of liability that is limited by section 34.4, the Admiralty Court, on application by that person or any other interested person, including a person who is a party to proceedings in relation to the same subject-matter before another court, tribunal or authority, may take any steps it considers appropriate, including

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: determining the amount of the liability and providing for the constitution and distribution of a fund under Articles 11 and 12 of the Convention;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: joining interested persons as parties to the proceedings, excluding any claimants who do not make a claim within a certain time, requiring security from the person claiming limitation of liability or from any other interested person and requiring the payment of any costs; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: enjoining any person from commencing or continuing proceedings in any court, tribunal or authority other than the Admiralty Court in relation to the same subject-matter.

Subsection.Text: In providing for the distribution of a fund under paragraph (1)(a) in relation to any liability, the Admiralty Court may, having regard to any claim that may subsequently be established before a court, tribunal or other authority outside Canada in respect of that liability, postpone the distribution of any part of the fund that it considers appropriate.
No lien or other right in respect of an air cushion vehicle or other property affects the proportions in which a fund is distributed by the Admiralty Court.
The Admiralty Court may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: make any rule of procedure it considers appropriate with respect to proceedings before it under this section; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: determine what form of guarantee it considers to be adequate for the purposes of paragraph 2 of Article 11 of the Convention.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of Article 11 of the Convention, interest is payable at the rate prescribed under the Income Tax Act for amounts payable by the Minister of National Revenue as refunds of overpayments of tax under that Act.

Section.Text: If an air cushion vehicle or other property is released under paragraph 2 of Article 13 of the Convention, in any case other than one in which a fund has been constituted in a place described in paragraphs 2(a) to (d) of that Article, the person who obtained the release is deemed to have submitted to the jurisdiction of the court that ordered the release for the purpose of determining the claim.
Paragraph 36(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the definition ship in Article 1 of the Convention shall be read as including any vessel, craft or air cushion vehicle designed, used or capable of being used solely or partly for navigation, whether seagoing or not, but not including a vessel propelled manually by paddles or oars; and

Section.Text: Section 41 of the Act is replaced by the following:

In this Part, Hague-Visby Rules means the rules set out in Schedule 3 and embodied in the International Convention for the Unification of Certain Rules of Law relating to Bills of Lading, concluded at Brussels on August 25, 1924, in the Protocol concluded at Brussels on February 23, 1968, and in the additional Protocol concluded at Brussels on December 21, 1979.
Subsection 43(4) of the Act is repealed.
Section 44 of the Act and the heading before it are repealed.
The portion of subsection 46(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If a contract for the carriage of goods by water provides for the adjudication or arbitration of claims arising under the contract in a place other than Canada, a claimant may institute judicial or arbitral proceedings in a court or arbitral tribunal in Canada that would be competent to determine the claim if the contract had referred the claim to Canada, if

Section.Text: Section 51 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Liability — fishing, hunting, trapping and harvesting
Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, for the purposes of Article III of the Civil Liability Convention, the liability of the owner of a ship includes economic loss in relation to the exercise of fishing, hunting, trapping or harvesting rights recognized and affirmed by section 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982 suffered by an Indigenous group, community or people that holds those rights or suffered by a member of such a group, community or people.

Section.Text: Subsection 53(1) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (a) and by adding the following after that paragraph:

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: permitting that person to reduce the amount that they are required to pay to the fund by up to an amount equal to the sum of any amounts paid to a claimant before the constitution of the fund; and

Section.Text: Subsections 54(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The person constituting the fund must, as soon as feasible, give notice of the fund’s constitution in

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Canada Gazette;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a newspaper in general circulation in the region where the incident occurred; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any other appropriate manner in the communities potentially affected by the incident.

Subsection.Text: Notices given under paragraphs (1)(b) and (c) are to be made accessible to the public for a period of at least 30 days.

The person constituting the fund must, within 15 days after the 30th day of the period referred to in subsection (1.1), file evidence in the Admiralty Court that the notices have been made accessible to the public for a period of at least 30 days.

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 55(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Unless a ship carries a certificate described in Article VII of the Civil Liability Convention issued in accordance with subsection 56(1), the ship must not

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following before section 69:
For the purposes of sections 69 to 74 and Articles 1 to 10 of the Bunkers Convention, the definition ship in Article 1 of the Bunkers Convention includes non-seagoing vessels and non-seaborne craft.
Section 71 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Liability — fishing, hunting, trapping and harvesting
Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, for the purposes of Article 3 of the Bunkers Convention, the liability of the owner of a ship includes economic loss in relation to the exercise of fishing, hunting, trapping or harvesting rights recognized and affirmed by section 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982 suffered by an Indigenous group, community or people that holds those rights or suffered by a member of such a group, community or people.

Section.Text: Section 72 of the Act is replaced by the following:
Division 1 of Part 3 applies to claims arising under the Bunkers Convention.
The portion of subsection 73(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Unless a ship carries a certificate described in Article 7 of the Bunkers Convention issued in accordance with subsection 74(1), the ship must not
Paragraph 77(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for oil pollution damage from the ship, including economic loss in relation to the exercise of fishing, hunting, trapping or harvesting rights recognized and affirmed by section 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982 suffered by an Indigenous group, community or people that holds those rights or suffered by a member of such a group, community or people;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 77(1.2) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:
For the purposes of subsection (1.1), words and expressions used in that subsection that are not defined have the same meaning as in the Civil Liability Convention as defined in subsection 47(1).
Subsection 92(2) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (c):

Paragraph.Label: (c.1)
Paragraph.Text: any amounts received by the Administrator further to the notice given under subsection 106.02(3) or paragraph 106.04(1)(b) or recovered under section 106.05;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 92(3)(a.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: all amounts that are directed to be paid under paragraph 106(3)(a) or 106.04(1)(c), subsection 106.3(4) or section 117 or under a settlement;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 92(3) of the Act is amended by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (d) and by repealing paragraph (e).
Section 103 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1.1):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Claims filed with Administrator — future losses
Subsection.Text: A claim arising out of oil pollution damage may be filed with the Administrator for

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: future loss of profit or income and future costs or expenses for the mitigation of that loss by a person who expects to suffer loss or incur costs or expenses;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: future economic loss related to fishing, hunting, trapping or harvesting for personal or household use and future costs or expenses for the mitigation of that loss by an individual who expects to suffer loss or incur costs or expenses;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: future economic loss related to the exercise of fishing, hunting, trapping or harvesting rights recognized and affirmed by section 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982 and future costs or expenses for the mitigation of that loss by a council, government or other entity that is authorized to act on behalf of an Indigenous group, community or people that holds those rights if that group, community or people expects to suffer loss or incur costs or expenses; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: costs or expenses for meeting the conditions imposed by the Administrator under subsection 105(1.2) by a person referred to in paragraph (a), an individual referred to in (b) or a council, government or other entity referred to in paragraph (c) who expects to incur those costs or expenses.

Subsection.Text: The claim filed under subsection (1.2) must specify the period covered by the claim, which, for greater certainty, may extend beyond the periods referred to in subsection (2) during which the claim must be filed.
The portion of subsection 103(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
A claim referred to in any of subsections (1) to (1.2) must be made
Paragraphs 103(2)(a) and (b) of the English version of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if oil pollution damage occurs, within two years after the day on which that damage occurs and five years after the incident that causes that damage; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if no oil pollution damage occurs, within five years after the incident in respect of which oil pollution damage is anticipated.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 103(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsections (1) to (1.2) do not apply to a person in a state other than Canada and subsections (1) and (1.1) do not apply to a response organization referred to in paragraph 51(1)(a), 71(1)(a) or 77(1)(b).
Subsection 105(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
On receipt of a claim under subsection 103(1) or (1.1), the Administrator shall investigate and assess it and shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: make an offer of compensation to the claimant for whatever portion of it that the Administrator finds to be established; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: disallow the claim and notify the claimant of its disallowance in writing.

Subsection.Text: On receipt of a claim under subsection 103(1.2), the Administrator shall investigate and assess it and shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: make an offer of compensation to the claimant for whatever portion of it that the Administrator finds to be established;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: make an offer of compensation to the claimant on an interim payment schedule for whatever portion of it that the Administrator finds to be established and provide the dates for future payments; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: disallow the claim and notify the claimant of its disallowance in writing.

Subsection.Text: If the Administrator makes an offer of compensation under paragraph (1.1)(a) or (b), the Administrator may impose conditions on the claimant as part of that offer, including a requirement to keep records containing information relating to the claim.
Paragraph 105(3)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: whether it is for loss, damage, costs or expenses referred to in subsection 103(1), (1.1) or (1.2), as the case may be; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 106(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If the Administrator makes an offer of compensation to a claimant under paragraph 105(1)(a), (1.1)(a) or (b), as the case may be, the claimant shall, within 60 days after receiving the offer, notify the Administrator whether they accept or refuse it and, if no notification is received by the Administrator within that period, the claimant is deemed to have refused the offer.
Subsection 106(2) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

A claimant may, within 60 days after receiving an offer of compensation or a notification that the Administrator has disallowed the claim, appeal the offer or the disallowance of the claim to the Admiralty Court, but in an appeal from the disallowance of a claim, that Court may consider only the matters described in paragraphs 105(3)(a) and (b).
Paragraphs 106(3)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Administrator shall direct payment to be made to the claimant of the amount of the offer out of the Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund without delay or according to the payment schedule referred to in paragraph 105(1.1)(b), as the case may be;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the claimant is then precluded from pursuing any rights that they may have had against any person in respect of matters referred to in sections 51, 71 and 77, Article III of the Civil Liability Convention and Article 3 of the Bunkers Convention in relation to the occurrence to which the offer of compensation relates, except that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: with respect to the acceptance of an offer of compensation made under paragraph 105(1)(a), the claimant may pursue any rights that they may have under subsection 103(1.2) in a single claim, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: with respect to the acceptance of an offer of compensation made under paragraph 105(1.1)(a) or (b), the claimant may pursue any rights that they may have under subsection 103(1) or (1.1) in a single claim;

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 106:

Subsection.Text: Despite paragraph 106(3)(a), the Administrator may direct, at any time after the claimant accepts an offer of compensation, that some or all of the payments referred to in paragraph 105(1.1)(b) not be made to the claimant if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a condition imposed on the claimant under subsection 105(1.2) is not met;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Administrator has reasonable grounds to believe that the claimant did not take reasonable measures to mitigate the loss for which a claim has been filed under subsection 103(1.2); or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the Administrator has reasonable grounds to believe that the claimant’s loss has been mitigated.

Subsection.Text: If the Administrator directs under subsection (1) that a payment not be made to the claimant, the Administrator must, as soon as feasible, give the claimant notice in writing.
A claimant may, within 60 days after receiving the notice, appeal the decision set out in the notice to the Admiralty Court.

If the Administrator imposes a condition requiring the claimant to keep records under subsection 105(1.2), the Administrator may, within the following periods, request that the claimant provide the Administrator with the records:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a payment made in accordance with an offer of compensation made under paragraph 105(1.1)(a), the period that ends on the later of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: one year after the day on which the payment was made, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: three years after the day of the occurrence in respect of which the claimant filed a claim;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of payments made in accordance with an offer of compensation made under paragraph 105(1.1)(b) that have ceased under subsection 106.01(1), the period that ends on the later of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: one year after the day on which the notice was sent by the Administrator under subsection 106.01(2), and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: three years after the day of the occurrence in respect of which the claimant filed a claim; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of payments made in accordance with an offer of compensation made under paragraph 105(1.1)(b) that have not ceased, the period that ends on the later of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: one year after the day on which the final payment was made, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: three years after the day of the occurrence in respect of which the claimant filed a claim.

Subsection.Text: If the Administrator requests records under subsection (1), the claimant must provide them to the Administrator within 30 days beginning on the day on which the claimant receives the request or within any longer period agreed to by the Administrator and the claimant.

If the claimant does not provide the Administrator with the records within the applicable period, the Administrator may give the claimant a notice that they are required to repay to the Administrator the amount or a portion of the amount of the payment made to them under paragraph 106(3)(a), in the manner and within the period set out in the notice.
The Administrator may, within the period referred to in subsection 106.02(1), investigate and reassess any claim under subsection 103(1.2) for which a payment was made under paragraph 106(3)(a).
For the purpose of investigating and reassessing a claim, the Administrator has the powers of a commissioner under Part I of the Inquiries Act.
When investigating and reassessing a claim, the Administrator may consider only

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: whether it is for loss, costs or expenses referred to in subsection 103(1.2) that the claimant has suffered or incurred;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: whether it resulted wholly or partially from

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an act done or omitted to be done by the claimant with intent to cause damage, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the claimant’s negligence;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: whether it is for loss, costs or expenses for which another claim has been filed;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: whether the Administrator has reasonable grounds to believe that the claimant did not take reasonable measures to mitigate the loss for which the claim has been filed;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: whether the claimant’s loss has been mitigated; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: whether the conditions imposed on the claimant under subsection 105(1.2), if any, have been met.

Subsection.Text: As soon as feasible after completing the investigation and reassessment, the Administrator must give the claimant notice that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Administrator will take no further action in respect of whatever portion of the claim was the subject of the investigation and reassessment;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the claimant is required to pay to the Administrator the overpayment set out in the notice, in the manner and within the period set out in the notice; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an additional payment, equal to the amount of the loss, costs or expenses suffered or incurred by the claimant minus the sum of any amounts already paid to the claimant, is to be made to the claimant without delay out of the Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund.

Subsection.Text: A claimant may, within 60 days after receiving a notice under paragraph (1)(b), appeal to the Admiralty Court the requirement to pay the overpayment set out in the notice.

Section.Text: All amounts payable under subsection 106.02(3) and overpayments payable under paragraph 106.04(1)(b) constitute debts due to His Majesty in right of Canada that may be recovered in a court of competent jurisdiction from the person who is required to pay them.
Paragraph 106.1(4)(a) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if oil pollution damage occurs, within one year after the day of the occurrence that causes that damage; or

Section.Text: The heading before section 107 and sections 107 and 108 of the Act are repealed.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 111.1(2) and (3) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Offer of compensation less than zero
Subsection.Text: If the offer of compensation made by the Administrator to the Minister of Fisheries and Oceans under paragraph 105(1)(a) is less than zero, then, unless that Minister appeals the offer under subsection 106(2), an amount equal to the amount of the offer is to be credited to the Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund, out of appropriations for the Department of Fisheries and Oceans under an appropriation Act, within six months after the day on which that Minister receives the offer or within any longer period agreed to by the Administrator and that Minister.

If the Minister of Fisheries and Oceans refuses the offer of compensation made under paragraph 105(1)(a), then, as soon as feasible after that Minister refuses the offer, an amount equal to the amount of the emergency funds charged to the Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund under subsection 110(1) or (2) is to be credited to the Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund, out of appropriations for the Department of Fisheries and Oceans under an appropriation Act, less any unused portion of the emergency funds credited to the Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund under subsection (1).
The description of A in subsection 111.1(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the portion of the claim that the Administrator finds to be established under subsection 105(1);

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 116(2)(b) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the claim is based on subsection 51(1) or 71(1) or paragraph 77(1)(b) or (c), or on Article III of the Civil Liability Convention or Article 3 of the Bunkers Convention as they pertain to preventive measures,

Subsection.Text: Subsection 116(2) of the Act is amended by adding “or” at the end of paragraph (a), by striking out “or” at the end of paragraph (b) and by repealing paragraph (c).

Section.Text: Schedule 4 to the Act is repealed.
The following definitions apply in sections 343 to 347.

Definition.Text: former Act means the Marine Liability Act as it read immediately before the day on which section 338 comes into force. (ancienne loi)
new Act means the Marine Liability Act as it reads on the day on which section 338 comes into force. (nouvelle loi)
Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund has the same meaning as in subsection 91(1) of the Marine Liability Act. (Caisse d’indemnisation)

Section.Text: A claim filed under section 107 of the former Act is to be dealt with in accordance with sections 107 and 108 of the former Act.

Despite subsection 103(2) of the new Act, if an occurrence in respect of which a person may file a claim under section 107 of the former Act takes place on or before the day on which section 338 comes into force, and if the limitation periods set out in subsection 107(3) of the former Act have not expired on that day, that person has the right to file a claim under subsection 103(1.2) of the new Act within those limitation periods.

Section.MarginalNote: Fund — amounts directed to be paid
Section.Text: Any amounts that are directed to be paid on or after the day on which subsection 332(2) comes into force, in respect of a claim filed under section 107 of the former Act, are to be charged to the Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund and they are deemed to be charged under paragraph 92(3)(a.1) of the new Act.

Section.MarginalNote: Fund — remuneration and expenses of assessors
Section.Text: The remuneration and expenses of assessors appointed under paragraph 108(2)(a) of the former Act that are directed to be paid on or after the day on which subsection 332(3) comes into force are to be charged to the Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund and are deemed to be charged under subsection 92(3) of the new Act.

If a payment is made to a claimant on or after the day on which subsection 340(2) comes into force, in respect of a claim filed under section 107 of the former Act, the claimant is entitled to the interest that accrues on a claim in accordance with subsection 116(1) of the Marine Liability Act from the time when the loss of income occurs.

Subsection.Text: In this section, other Act means the Safeguarding Canada’s Seas and Skies Act.
On the first day on which both section 33 of the other Act and section 313 of this Act are in force, paragraph 33(1)(a.1) of the Marine Liability Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: permitting that person to reduce the amount that they are required to pay to the fund by up to an amount equal to the sum of any amounts paid to a claimant before the constitution of the fund in respect of a maritime claim for oil pollution damage caused by bunker oil as defined in paragraph 5 of Article 1 of the Bunkers Convention as defined in subsection 47(1);

Subsection.Text: On the first day on which both section 33 of the other Act and section 324 of this Act are in force, subsection 74.26(1) of the Marine Liability Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (a) and by adding the following after that paragraph:

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: permitting that person to reduce the amount that they are required to pay to the fund by up to an amount equal to the sum of any amounts paid to a claimant before the constitution of the fund; and

Subsection.Text: On the first day on which both section 33 of the other Act and section 325 of this Act are in force, subsections 74.27(1) and (2) of the Marine Liability Act are replaced by the following:
The person constituting the fund must, as soon as feasible, give notice of the fund’s constitution in

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Canada Gazette;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a newspaper in general circulation in the region where the incident occurred; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any other appropriate manner in the communities potentially affected by the incident.

Subsection.Text: Notices given under paragraphs (1)(b) and (c) are to be made accessible to the public for a period of at least 30 days.

The person constituting the fund must, within 15 days after the 30th day of the period referred to in subsection (1.1), file evidence in the Admiralty Court that the notices have been made accessible to the public for a period of at least 30 days.
On the first day on which both subsection 45(1) of the other Act and subsection 335(3) of this Act are in force, paragraph 106(3)(b) of the Marine Liability Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the claimant is then precluded from pursuing any rights that they may have had, other than in relation to damage as defined in paragraph 6(a) of Article 1 of the Hazardous and Noxious Substances Convention, against any person in respect of matters referred to in sections 51, 71, 74.24 and 77, Article III of the Civil Liability Convention, Article 3 of the Bunkers Convention and Article 7 of the Hazardous and Noxious Substances Convention in relation to the occurrence to which the offer of compensation relates, except that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: with respect to the acceptance of an offer of compensation made under paragraph 105(1)(a), the claimant may pursue any rights that they may have under subsection 103(1.2) in a single claim, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: with respect to the acceptance of an offer of compensation made under paragraph 105(1.1)(a) or (b), the claimant may pursue any rights that they may have under subsection 103(1) or (1.1) in a single claim;

Subsection.Text: On the first day on which both subsection 49(2) of the other Act and subsection 340(1) of this Act are in force, the portion of paragraph 116(2)(b) of the Marine Liability Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the claim is based on subsection 51(1), 71(1) or 74.24(1) or paragraph 77(1)(b) or (c), or on Article III of the Civil Liability Convention, Article 3 of the Bunkers Convention or Article 7 of the Hazardous and Noxious Substances Convention as they pertain to preventive measures,

Subsection.Text: In this section, other Act means the Budget Implementation Act, 2018, No. 2.

On the first day on which both section 323 of this Act is in force and subsection 746(2) of the other Act has produced its effects, section 74.24 of the Marine Liability Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Liability — fishing, hunting, trapping and harvesting
Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, for the purposes of Article 7 of the Hazardous and Noxious Substances Convention, the liability of the owner of a ship includes economic loss in relation to the exercise of fishing, hunting, trapping or harvesting rights recognized and affirmed by section 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982 suffered by an Indigenous group, community or people that holds those rights or suffered by a member of such a group, community or people.

On the first day on which both subsection 333(3) of this Act is in force and subsection 746(8) of the other Act has produced its effects, subsection 103(3) of the Marine Liability Act is replaced by the following:

Subsections (1) to (1.2) do not apply to a person in a state other than Canada, and subsections (1) and (1.1) do not apply to a response organization referred to in paragraph 51(1)(a), 71(1)(a), 74.24(1)(a) or 77(1)(b).

Section.Text: Section 2 of the Canada Shipping Act, 2001 is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: hazardous and noxious substances handling facility means a facility that is used or that will be used in the loading or unloading of hazardous and noxious substances to or from vessels. (installation de manutention de substances nocives et potentiellement dangereuses)
port authority has the same meaning as in subsection 2(1) of the Canada Marine Act. (administration portuaire)

Subsection.Text: The portion of section 8 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: This Part applies in respect of Canadian vessels everywhere, in respect of foreign vessels in Canadian waters, and in respect of pleasure craft that are not Canadian vessels but that are in Canadian waters or in waters in the exclusive economic zone of Canada. However

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 8(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 10(2.1) and 35.1(1) also apply in respect of foreign vessels in waters in the exclusive economic zone of Canada.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 10(1)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: enter into agreements or arrangements respecting the administration or enforcement of any provision of this Act, the regulations or an interim order made under subsection 10.1(1) or (1.1) and authorize any person or organization — including a provincial government, a local authority and a government, council or other entity authorized to act on behalf of an Indigenous group — with whom or which an agreement or arrangement is entered into to exercise the powers or perform the duties and functions under this Act that are specified in the agreement or arrangement.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 10(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Minister of Transport or the Minister of Fisheries and Oceans may, with respect to that Minister’s responsibilities under this Act, exempt for a specified period any authorized representative, person, class of persons, master, vessel, class of vessels, operator of an oil handling facility, oil handling facility or class of oil handling facilities, operator of a hazardous and noxious substances handling facility, hazardous and noxious substances handling facility or class of hazardous and noxious substances handling facilities from the application of any provision of this Act, the regulations or an interim order made under subsection 10.1(1) or (1.1), subject to any conditions that that Minister considers appropriate, if that Minister is of the opinion that the exemption is in the interest of preventing damage to property or the environment or is in the public interest or interest of public health or safety.
Section 10.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

The Minister of Transport may, subject to any restrictions or conditions that the Minister may specify, authorize the Deputy Minister of Transport to make, for the purpose referred to in subsection (1), an interim order that contains any provision that may be contained in a regulation made, under this Act, on the recommendation of only that Minister.
The portion of subsection 10.1(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
An interim order made under this section has effect from the time that it is made or from any later date that may be specified in the interim order, but ceases to have effect on the earliest of
Paragraph 10.1(2)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: one year after the effective date of the interim order or any shorter period that may be specified in the interim order, unless the effective period is extended by the Minister of Transport or the Governor in Council;

Paragraph.Label: (c.1)
Paragraph.Text: the day that is specified in the order of the Minister of Transport extending the interim order, if the Minister extends the effective period of the interim order, unless the effective period is extended by the Governor in Council; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 10.1(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If the period specified in the interim order is less than one year, the Minister of Transport may extend the effective period of the interim order for a period that ends no more than one year after the effective date.

The Governor in Council may extend the effective period of the interim order for a period of no more than two years after the end of the applicable period referred to in paragraph (2)(c) or after the day referred to in paragraph (2)(c.1).
Subsection 10.1(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Statutory Instruments Act does not apply to an interim order or an order extending the interim order. However, any such order must be published in the Canada Gazette within 23 days after it is made.

Section.Text: Subsection 12(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Minister of Transport may authorize any person, class of persons, classification society or other organization to issue any Canadian maritime document under this Act or to carry out inspections under section 211 if the Minister determines that the person, class of persons, classification society or other organization is qualified to issue the document or carry out the inspection.

Section.Text: Subsections 14(2) and (3) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Qualified person, bare-boat charterer or owner
Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (3) and (4), the authorized representative of a Canadian vessel is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a vessel whose owner has entered into an arrangement with a qualified person, including an operator of the vessel, under which they are responsible for the matters referred to in subsection (1), the qualified person;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a vessel described in section 48, the bare-boat charterer; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, the owner of the vessel.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Representative if more than one owner
Subsection.Text: In the case of a Canadian vessel that is owned by more than one person, the owners must appoint as the authorized representative one of themselves or, in accordance with paragraph (2)(a), a qualified person.

Section.Text: Section 21 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Issuance of documents to foreign vessels
